segments.p2-8-g1: 'Я' is the nominative personal pronoun 'I' and functions as the sentence subject; its initial position with a comma gives slight emphasis before the parenthetical 'впрочем'.
segments.p2-8-g2: 'впрочем' is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'however' or 'anyway' that modifies the whole clause; here it signals a contrast with something said earlier.
segments.p2-8-g3: This clause contains the negated adverbial 'не скоро' ('not soon / not quickly'), the past-tense perfective verb 'согласился' (masculine singular) meaning 'agreed/consented', followed by the infinitive 'признать' ('to admit/acknowledge') and the accusative object 'эту истину' ('this truth'). The structure means 'was not quick to agree to admit this truth.'
segments.p2-8-g4: 'Проснувшись' is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) from 'проснуться' meaning 'having woken'; it sets the temporal context for the main clause. 'наутро' here means 'in the morning' and 'после нескольких часов глубокого' is a prepositional phrase 'after several hours of deep...' with 'глубокого' in the genitive agreeing with the omitted noun 'сна' that follows in the next segment.
segments.p2-8-g5: 'свинцового сна' is a genitive phrase 'of leaden (heavy) sleep' where 'свинцового' is an adjective modifying 'сна'. The conjunction 'и' links the participial phrase to 'тотчас же сообразив' — another gerund 'сообразив' ('having realized/figured out') meaning the narrator immediately took in the events; 'тотчас же' is an emphatic adverbial 'at once/immediately'. 'весь вчерашний день' is the accusative/accusative-like object of 'сообразив' — 'the whole of yesterday.'
segments.p2-8-g6: Main clause: 'я' (subject) + 'даже' ('even', adverb of emphasis) + 'изумился' (past reflexive verb 'was astonished'). The phrase that follows uses the dative 'моей вчерашней сантиментальности' because 'изумиться' governs the dative ('to be astonished at something'), and 'с Лизой' (instrumental) indicates the person involved — 'my sentimentalism toward/with Liza.'
segments.p2-8-g7: This is an extension of the object of 'изумился' in the dative plural: 'всем этим' ('at all these') + 'вчерашним ужасам и жалостям' ('yesterday's horrors and pity'). Grammatically the whole phrase is in the dative to indicate what astonished him ('he was astonished at all these...').
segments.p2-8-g8: 'Ведь' is a discourse particle 'after all' that strengthens the following statement. 'Нападет' is future tense, 3rd-person singular of 'напасть' used here in the intransitive/sudden sense 'will seize/befall' (i.e., 'will set in'). 'же' is an emphatic particle attached to the verb. The noun phrase 'такое бабье расстройство нервов' ('such a woman's/womanish disorder of the nerves') is the subject/predicate phrase describing what will occur; 'бабье' is pejorative/colloquial 'womanish' or 'women's' and 'нервов' is the genitive 'of the nerves.'
segments.p2-8-g9: 'тьфу' is an interjection (onomatopoeic) used to express disgust, scorn, or to mimic a spitting sound; here it punctuates the dismissive remark and is typically translated as 'pah' or 'ugh.'
segments.p2-8-g10: Inverted short clause: 'порешил' is past masculine singular of colloquial 'порешить' meaning 'decided/settled' (here sense 'resolved/decided'), and the pronoun 'я' follows for emphasis. The tone is colloquial; the verb is perfective past.
segments.p2-8-g24: Entire sentence = demonstrative particle + prepositional clause + past copula + predicate. Вот is a pointing particle ('this/that is'); в чем is a prepositional phrase 'in what' (чем = interrogative/relative pronoun in the prepositional case); было is the past tense neuter of быть used as a copula agreeing with the neuter predicate; главное дело is the neuter nominal predicate 'the main matter/point.' Word order (Вот в чем было главное дело) is idiomatic and stresses the clause introduced by вот.
segments.p2-8-g25: А introduces a contrast or transition ('and/but'). Про Лизу uses the preposition про + accusative to mean 'about/concerning Liza.' Я — subject 'I.' Даже совсем и is a string of emphatics ('even completely / quite'); the second и here is a colloquial emphatic particle rather than a simple conjunction. Забыл is past masculine singular 'forgot', and в это утро uses в + demonstrative это + утро to mark the specific time 'that morning.'
segments.p2-8-g26: Захлопотавшись is a perfective de‑епричастие (gerund) meaning 'having become busy' or 'having begun to bustle' and expresses the cause or attendant circumstance for the main clause (it explains why he forgot). It is formed from the perfective verb and implies a completed (or initiated) action concurrent with or immediately preceding the main verb.
segments.p2-8-g27: Прежде всего is a fixed phrase meaning 'first of all.' Надо было is an impersonal past construction ('it was necessary' / 'I had to'), where было marks past time and the infinitive отдать functions as the required action. Немедленно is an adverb 'immediately.' Вчерашний modifies долг: 'yesterday's debt.' Симонову is the dative form indicating the recipient of the repayment. Word order places the necessity phrase before the action for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g28: Я решился uses the reflexive verb решиться in past tense meaning 'I decided' or 'I dared' (to undertake something). На + accusative (на отчаянное средство) indicates the object or course chosen ('on/for a desperate means/expedient'). Отчаянное is neuter nominative/accusative singular agreeing with средство and characterizes the means as desperate.
segments.p2-8-g29: Занять is an infinitive complement meaning 'to borrow' here; целых is an emphatic adjective 'a full/no less than' (expressing surprise at the amount); пятнадцать рублей is the numeral + genitive plural required after numerals; у + genitive (у Антона Антоновича) indicates the person 'from Anton Antonovich' (literally 'at Anton's' or 'from Anton's possession'); the whole phrase names the desperate act the narrator resolved to commit.
segments.p2-8-g11: "И" is a discourse conjunction here ('and') introducing the question; "на что" is the colloquial interrogative 'what for / to what end' (literally 'on what'); "это" is a filler/emphatic demonstrative placed before the noun; word order "всучил я ей" (verb–subject–dative) puts the past-tense verb всучил (perfective, 'foisted/slipped') before the pronoun я for emphasis, and ей is the dative object indicating the person who received the address.
segments.p2-8-g12: Что here is an introductory/question particle 'what' (= 'what if...'), used colloquially to introduce a hypothetical.
segments.p2-8-g13: "если" is the conditional conjunction 'if'; "она" is the 3rd-person pronoun 'she'; "придет" is the perfective future 3rd-person singular of прийти ('will come/comes'), forming the protasis of the hypothetical question.
segments.p2-8-g14: "А" functions as an adversative conjunction ('but' / 'well'), and "впрочем" is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'however' or 'on the other hand', used to soften or revise the previous thought.
segments.p2-8-g15: Пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', marking a tentative or conciliatory attitude.
segments.p2-8-g16: Пусть is an optative/imperative particle meaning 'let (her)...' (permission/acceptance); the following и is an intensifier ('even'/'also') and придет is future indicative ('comes'/'will come'); together they express resigned allowance ('let her even come').
segments.p2-8-g17: Ничего here is idiomatic: literally 'nothing', but used elliptically to mean 'it doesn't matter' or 'never mind' — a dismissive comment closing the sentence.
segments.p2-8-g20: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but/however', linking this sentence to what came before and signaling a change of emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g21: Очевидно is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'obviously' or 'apparently', commenting on the speaker's assessment.
segments.p2-8-g22: Главное и самое важное дело is a nominal phrase 'the main and most important matter' (главное and самое важное both modify дело); теперь 'now' positions the time; было is the past neuter copula agreeing with the neuter noun дело; не в этом is a prepositional phrase 'was not in this' idiomatically meaning 'that wasn't the point.'
segments.p2-8-g23: Надо было is an impersonal past-tense modal construction meaning 'it was necessary / I had to'; спешить is the infinitive 'to hurry'; и links the coordinated action; во что бы ни стало is an idiomatic phrase ('whatever it might cost' / 'at any cost') formed from во + что + бы (conditional particle) + ни (negative/emphatic particle) + стало (part of the fixed phrase); скорее modifies спасать as 'more quickly' / 'as soon as possible'; спасать is imperfective 'to save' indicating an action the speaker needs to undertake; мою репутацию is the accusative object 'my reputation'; в глазах + genitive names expresses 'in the eyes/views of Zverkov and Simonov.'
segments.p2-8-g126: An interjection of hesitation or thought (written with a period here). It functions independently and carries no grammatical inflection; it's equivalent to English "hm/hmm."
segments.p2-8-g129: "ну" is a colloquial particle used to introduce or soften a statement; combined with "нет" it yields a dismissive or corrective "well, no." This is discourse-level (not syntactic) and prepares the correction in the next phrase.
segments.p2-8-g130: "не" negates the following prepositional phrase; "от" as a preposition here has a causal sense "because of/from" and "вина" is the genitive of "вино" (wine). Together they mean "not because of the wine."
segments.p2-8-g131: "Водки" is the genitive used with negation to indicate absence/partitive ("any vodka"). The enclitic particle "-то" on "Водки-то" adds contrastive emphasis ("as for vodka"). "вовсе не пил" is a strong negation in the past (masculine singular) meaning "did not drink at all." The subject "я" is explicit here.
segments.p2-8-g132: A time-span introduced by the preposition pair "от...до..." ("from...to..."). "пяти" is in the genitive as required after "от," "-то" again adds emphasis to "пяти," and "шести часов" marks the end point; the whole phrase means "from five to six o'clock."
segments.p2-8-g133: "когда" is the subordinating conjunction "when," introducing the temporal clause. "их" is the accusative/plural pronoun "them," the object of "поджидал," and "поджидал" is the past tense of "поджидать" meaning "to wait for/await" (here simple waiting rather than ambush). The clause ties the time span to the action of waiting for them.
segments.p2-8-g134: "Солгал" is the past-tense masculine form of "солгать/лгать" meaning "lied." The dative "Симонову" marks the person to whom the lie was told ("to Simonov"). The subject "I" is omitted but understood from context.
segments.p2-8-g135: A repetition of the verb with an adverb of manner: "солгал бессовестно" = "lied shamelessly." The subject remains omitted (same "I"); the adverb "бессовестно" modifies how the lying was done.
segments.p2-8-g136: "да и" is a colloquial connective meaning roughly "and besides/and even," linking the statement to what follows. "теперь" = "now," and "не совестно" negates the adjective/adverbial notion of feeling conscience-stricken; together it means "and even now I don't feel ashamed" or "it's not shameful even now."
segments.p2-8-g139: 'А' is a short connective/interjection often rendered 'ah' or 'but' and here sets a colloquial, dismissive tone before the adverb; 'впрочем' is an adverb meaning 'anyway/however' and together they form an interjection roughly 'ah, well' or 'but anyway'. Word order is the normal sequence: particle + adverbic modifier.
segments.p2-8-g140: Наплевать is a colloquial perfective verb used as an exclamation meaning 'to not care' (literally 'to spit on'); here it stands alone as the main sentence, expressing strong indifference. No explicit subject is needed (first‑person sense is understood from context).
segments.p2-8-g141: Главное is a neuter nominalized adjective meaning 'the main thing' (used as a noun). То here is a demonstrative/particle pointing forward to the subordinate clause, functioning like English 'is that'. Together they form the topical phrase 'The main thing is'.
segments.p2-8-g142: Что introduces the subordinate clause ('that ...'). Отделался is the past tense, perfective, reflexive verb meaning 'got off with/was rid of' and agrees implicitly with the (omitted) subject of the main clause (the narrator, masculine). The reflexive suffix -ся signals the meaning 'escape/get away (with)'.
segments.p2-8-g143: Я is the nominative subject 'I'. Вложил is the past tense, perfective verb 'inserted/put into' (action completed). В + письмо is the preposition + accusative 'into the letter', and шесть рублей is the direct object of вложил expressed as a cardinal numeral with the genitive 'рублей' (standard Russian case after numerals). Word order is Subject–Verb–prepositional phrase–object.
segments.p2-8-g144: This is a coordinate predicate sequence: запечатал (sealed) and упросил (begged) are past perfective verbs sharing the same subject (Я). The direct object of both verbs (the letter) is omitted because it's understood from the prior clause. Упросил takes a person in accusative (Аполлона) as the one he begged; снести is an infinitive complement meaning 'to bring/take (there)' and is governed by упросил; к + dative (Симонову) indicates destination 'to Simonov'.
segments.p2-8-g145: Узнав is the perfective adverbial participle (деепричастие) 'having found out' (action prior to the main clause). It sets the temporal/circumstantial background for the main clause that follows.
segments.p2-8-g146: Что introduces the content clause 'that ...'. В письме is the prepositional phrase 'in the letter' (в + prepositional case). Деньги is the nominative plural subject of the subordinate clause, meaning 'money' (general funds, not individual coins).
segments.p2-8-g147: Аполлон is the subject (a man's name). Стал is the past tense verb 'became' and is followed by the comparative adverb почтительнее 'more respectful' describing a change of manner; и coordinates the next verb согласился 'agreed/consented', which takes the infinitive сходить ('to go' with the nuance 'go and come back' or 'make the short trip') as its complement. The sequence describes cause (finding money) → change of attitude → consent to act.
segments.p2-8-g30: Как нарочно is an idiomatic adverbial phrase meaning something like "as if arranged on purpose" or "as if providentially/opportunely." It modifies the whole clause that follows, adding a tone of ironic coincidence.
segments.p2-8-g31: The clause places temporal phrase "в это утро" ("that morning/on that very morning") before the verb for emphasis: "он был в это утро..." (he was that morning...). "В прекраснейшем расположении духа" is a fixed expression (prepositional case) meaning "in the best of spirits; in excellent humor;" the adjective "прекраснейшем" is an older/highly emphatic superlative form. "И тотчас же выдал" uses the past tense perfective verb "выдал" (he gave/handed out) with an omitted direct object (understood: the money), and "тотчас же" is an emphatic "immediately."
segments.p2-8-g32: "По первой просьбе" is a short prepositional phrase with "по + dative" meaning "on the first request" or "at the first asking." "Первой" is dative singular feminine agreeing with "просьбе." It completes the verb "выдал" (he handed [it] over on the first request).
segments.p2-8-g33: "Я так этому обрадовался" — "Я" is the subject; "так" intensifies the degree ("so"); "этому" is a dative demonstrative referring back to the preceding fact ("about that/this"); "обрадовался" is the past perfective reflexive verb meaning "was glad/was delighted." Together: "I was so glad about that."
segments.p2-8-g34: The conjunction "что" introduces a subordinate clause ("that..."), linking the preceding emotional statement to the action that follows.
segments.p2-8-g35: "Подписывая расписку" is a present active participial phrase (imperfective) meaning "while signing the IOU" or "as I signed the IOU," indicating simultaneous action with the main clause.
segments.p2-8-g36: "С каким-то ухарским видом" is an instrument/description phrase: "с" + instrumental noun phrase meaning "with a sort of/with some rakish/gallant air." "Каким-то" (here split as "каким" + "то") is an indefinite modifier meaning "some kind of/what a...," and "ухарским видом" uses the instrumental "вид" (appearance/air).
segments.p2-8-g37: "Небрежно сообщил ему" is the main verb phrase: "небрежно" (adverb) "carelessly," "сообщил" (past tense) "informed/told," and "ему" (dative) "to him." The participial phrase preceding it sets the manner/timing: "while signing... I carelessly told him."
segments.p2-8-g38: "Что вчера 'покутили с приятелями в Hotel de Paris'" introduces quoted reported speech. "Что" = "that," "вчера" = "yesterday," and "покутили" is perfective past plural meaning "we caroused/was carousing" (colloquial). The quotation marks indicate the narrator is reproducing what he told Anton Antonovich.
segments.p2-8-g39: "Провожали товарища" continues the quoted account: imperfective past plural "провожали" = "we were seeing off/escorting," and "товарища" is the accusative of "товарищ" (a comrade/companion).
segments.p2-8-g40: "Даже" is a particle meaning "even" and adds emphasis to what follows.
segments.p2-8-g41: "Можно сказать" is an idiomatic parenthetical: literally "one can say," i.e. "you might say/so to speak," softening or qualifying the following claim.
segments.p2-8-g42: "Друга детства" is an accusative phrase "a friend of childhood" ("a childhood friend"). It completes the prior verb "провожали" (we were seeing off a comrade — even, you might say, a childhood friend).
segments.p2-8-g43: "И" is the simple coordinating conjunction "and," linking the next descriptive clause.
segments.p2-8-g44: "Знаете" here is the conversational filler "you know," used to address the listener and draw attention; grammatically it's second person singular present but used pragmatically as a discourse marker.
segments.p2-8-g45: "Кутила он большой" is a colloquial phrase: "кутила" (noun) "a hard-partying fellow/a roué," followed by "он" (he) and the predicate adjective "большой" meaning "great/considerable" in the evaluative sense — "he was quite a reveler." The word order is slightly colloquial/elliptical.
segments.p2-8-g46: "Избалован" is a short passive/adjectival past participle meaning "spoiled/pampered." It continues the string of character descriptors applied to the man in the quoted speech.
segments.p2-8-g47: "- ну," is an interjectional hesitation/colloquial filler — "well," used to stall or introduce the next qualifying remarks.
segments.p2-8-g48: "Разумеется" is an adverb meaning "of course" and signals that the following attributes are expected or conventional.
segments.p2-8-g49: "Хорошей фамилии" is a genitive attribute phrase: literally "of a good family/name." In context it means "from a good family" (i.e. of reputable birth). "Хорошей" is feminine genitive singular agreeing with "фамилии."
segments.p2-8-g50: "Значительное состояние" is a noun phrase meaning "a considerable fortune/estate." "Значительное" modifies "состояние," here taken in the sense of wealth (dative/accusative nominal role within the list of attributes).
segments.p2-8-g51: "Блестящая карьера" is another attributive phrase meaning "a brilliant/shining career." The adjective is figurative ("brilliant") and "карьера" is the noun "career."
segments.p2-8-g52: "Остроумен" is a short predicate adjective meaning "witty." It's one-word description continuing the list of flattering traits.
segments.p2-8-g53: "Мил" is another brief predicate adjective meaning "pleasant/amiable/dear," part of the quick catalogue of the man's qualities.
segments.p2-8-g54: "Интригует с этими дамами" is a present-tense clause "he intrigues/plots with those ladies," colloquially meaning "he flirts with those ladies." "Интригует" is present indicative, "с" + instrumental marks the people with whom he intrigues.
segments.p2-8-g55: "Понимаете:" is the present second-person plural/formal "you understand" used rhetorically as a tag to make sure the listener follows; the colon marks an explanation or example following it.
segments.p2-8-g56: "Выпили лишних 'полдюжины' и." is colloquial and elliptical: "выпили" (past plural) "we drank/had," "лишних" = "an extra/too many," "полдюжины" = "half-dozen." The final "и" trails off, indicating the speaker cut off or left the story unfinished. The quoted speech thus ends in an ellipsis.
segments.p2-8-g59: И is the coordinating conjunction 'and' placed at the clause start. Ведь is a particle meaning 'after all' or 'you know', adding a dismissive/emphatic tone. Ничего is the indefinite pronoun 'nothing' used elliptically here (the verb is omitted): the whole clause means roughly 'and, after all, nothing (of consequence).'
segments.p2-8-g60: Произносилось is the past tense, reflexive/passive form of произносить ('was said/was pronounced'); here it is neuter singular, matching the following neuter phrase все это. The verb is placed before its subject (все это), a common stylistic order. Всё это is 'all this' (всё with the neuter demonstrative это). Очень modifies the adverb легко, meaning 'very easily' or 'very lightly.'
segments.p2-8-g61: Развязно and самодовольно are adverbial manner words describing how the speaking occurred; they are coordinated by и. Развязно carries a sense of impudence/brazen manner; самодовольно means 'self‑satisfiedly' or 'smugly.' They both modify the preceding verb phrase 'произносилось.'
segments.p2-8-g62: Придя is the adverbial past participle (деепричастие) of прийти, meaning 'having come' or 'on coming.' Домой is a directional adverb meaning 'home' or 'to/towards home.' Together they form an adverbial clause of time setting when the main action occurred.
segments.p2-8-g63: Я is the subject pronoun 'I.' Немедленно is an adverb 'immediately' modifying the verb. Написал is the past tense, perfective verb 'wrote' (here specifically 'wrote [a letter]'). Симонову is the dative form of the surname, marking the recipient of the letter: 'wrote to Simonov.' The adverbial participle Придя relates to the same subject (я).
segments.p2-8-g169: Subject Я + the negated modal construction никак не мог + infinitive справиться. Никак не мог is an emphatic negation meaning 'could not in any way.' The infinitive with с собой (себя in instrumental) means 'manage/cope with oneself' — справиться is perfective 'to manage/cope'.
segments.p2-8-g170: Концов is genitive plural of конец used idiomatically (partitive/genitive) in the phrase 'концов найти' meaning 'to find an end/solution' or 'to make sense of things.' Найти is the infinitive 'to find/discover.' The second clause is elliptical/paratactic and completes the thought of the first negative clause.
segments.p2-8-g171: Что-то is the indefinite pronoun 'something' (что + то treated as neuter), serving as the subject. Подымалось is past-tense, imperfective, neuter singular to agree with the neuter pronoun; the repetition подымалось, подымалось gives emphatic continuous action 'kept rising.'
segments.p2-8-g172: Repeated verb подымалось continues the same action; в душе is a prepositional phrase 'in (my) soul/heart' (душе in prepositional case) indicating location/experiential center. Беспрерывно is an adverb modifying the verb: 'continuously/without pause.'
segments.p2-8-g173: С + instrumental болью expresses manner 'with pain' or 'painfully.' The short phrase with comma is an adverbial modifier of the rising action.
segments.p2-8-g174: И links to the previous clause; не хотело is past tense neuter singular of хотеть agreeing with the neuter subject 'что-то' and means 'did not want.' Угомониться is a perfective reflexive infinitive 'to calm down/quiet down,' so the whole phrase means 'and would not calm down.'
segments.p2-8-g175: Совсем расстроенный is a preposed participial/adjectival phrase ('completely upset/distraught') modifying the subject я. Воротился is past tense (reflexive) 'returned' and домой is a directional adverb 'homewards' (to home). The word order places the participle before the pronoun for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g176: Точно как будто is a double comparative/simile 'exactly/as if'; как будто both reinforce the hypothetical comparison. На душе моей is a locative/prepositional phrase 'on my soul' (душе in prepositional case with possessive моей). Лежало is past neuter singular 'lay' and agrees with the neuter subject какое-то преступление ('some kind of crime'), where какое-то is the indefinite 'some/such' and преступление is 'crime.'
segments.p2-8-g114: Любовался is a past-tense, masculine, reflexive verb (любоваться) meaning 'to admire' or 'to gaze with pleasure'. The verb precedes the pronoun я (inverted word order) for emphasis: literally 'admired I'. The dash at the sentence start marks direct speech.
segments.p2-8-g115: Перечитывая is a деепричастие (adverbial participle) formed from the imperfective verb перечитывать, meaning 'while/as rereading' and supplying the circumstance for the main action. Записку is the accusative singular of записка, the direct object of перечитывая ('the note').
segments.p2-8-g116: А is a connective/conjunctive particle here ('and'/'but') introducing contrast; всё functions adverbially as an intensifier ('it's all' / 'all of it'); оттого is an adverb meaning 'because of that' and normally introduces a clause with что. Together they mean 'and it's all because...'.
segments.p2-8-g117: Что introduces the explanatory clause ('that' / 'because'). Развитой и образованный are two adjectives in nominative masculine modifying человек; the noun phrase is presented as an exclamation with the copula omitted (implied 'I am'). Word order is adjective–adjective–noun, and the whole phrase is quoted/exclaimed as the reason.
segments.p2-8-g118: Другие is plural 'others' (subject). Бы is the conditional particle that combines with the past plural verb знали to form 'would have known'. На моём месте is a locative/figurative prepositional phrase 'in my place/in my position'. Не знали is negated past plural 'would not have known'.
segments.p2-8-g119: Как is an interrogative/relative adverb here meaning 'how'. И is an adding/emphatic particle ('even/also'), and выпутаться is the infinitive reflexive 'to extricate oneself' acting as the complement of 'не знали' ('wouldn't have known how to extricate themselves').
segments.p2-8-g120: А contrasts with the previous clause ('but'). Я is the subject. Вот is a discourse particle ('look/you see' / 'well') adding immediacy. Вывернулся is past tense reflexive 'wriggled free / managed to get out (of it)'. The phrase 'и кучу себе вновь' is elliptical: кучу (accusative 'a heap' used colloquially as 'a mess' or 'a lot of trouble') with себе ('for myself') and вновь ('again') has an implied verb ('made'/'got') omitted for stylistic effect.
segments.p2-8-g121: И here links and reinforces the causal remark; всё is emphatic ('all/it all'); потому is an adverb meaning 'for that reason' and prepares for the following explanatory что-clause.
segments.p2-8-g122: Что again introduces the explanatory phrase (here quoted). 'Образованный и развитой человек нашего времени' is a nominal phrase in nominative with two adjectives and a genitive attribute нашего времени ('of our time'), functioning as the sarcastic reason quoted by the narrator.
segments.p2-8-g123: Да is an interjection here ('yes'/'indeed'); combined with и it forms the colloquial intensifier 'да и' ('and besides' / 'indeed'), while впрямь is an adverb 'indeed/really' emphasizing assent or confirmation.
segments.p2-8-g124: Пожалуй is a parenthetical modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', used to soften or hedge the speaker's statement.
segments.p2-8-g125: Это всё is the subject ('this all' / 'all this'); от governs the genitive вина and here means 'because of' (causal); вчера is a temporal adverb 'yesterday'; произошло is the past neuter of произойти ('happen/occur'), neuter agreeing with the pronoun это. Word order places the causal phrase before the verb for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g185: "Точно как будто" is an emphatic preface meaning "exactly/as if," introducing a comparison; "я" is nominative subject "I"; "одной Лизой" is instrumental singular with "одной" (= "alone/only") indicating that Liza is the sole agent or cause of the torment; "и" here is a colloquial/emphatic particle rather than a simple conjunction; "мучился" is the masculine past reflexive verb (imperfective) meaning "was tormented/agonized."
segments.p2-8-g186: Single-word utterance "Что," functions as an introductory/exclamatory particle here (roughly "what/now then"), leading into a conditional question; the following clause completes the idea.
segments.p2-8-g187: "если" is the subordinating conjunction "if"; "она" is the 3rd-person feminine pronoun subject "she"; "придет" is the perfective future, 3rd person singular of "прийти" meaning "will come/arrive," forming the conditional question "if she comes?"
segments.p2-8-g188: "Думал" is past tense, masculine, imperfective of "думать" meaning "thought/was thinking" and here conveys repeated or continuous mental action; the inversion "думал я" places the verb before the pronoun for emphasis; "беспрерывно" is an adverb "continuously/without interruption" modifying the verb.
segments.p2-8-g189: "Ну что ж" is a fixed colloquial phrase meaning "well then" or "well, then," where "ну" is an interjection, "что" works as an exclamatory/introductory particle, and "ж" is an enclitic particle adding emphasis (roughly "after all").
segments.p2-8-g190: "Ничего" here is idiomatic and functions as "never mind" or "it's nothing" — a dismissive response preparing to allow the proposed event.
segments.p2-8-g191: "Пусть" is the particle used to give permission or concede ("let (her)"), "и" is an emphatic particle here ("even/anyway"), and "придет" is the future 3rd-person singular of "прийти" — together "let her come (then)."
segments.p2-8-g148: К вечеру is a temporal prepositional phrase: к + dative (вечеру) = 'by/toward evening' indicating approximate time. Вышел is the masculine singular past tense of выйти ('to go out'); the following infinitive пройтись expresses purpose ('went out in order to take a walk'). Word order is normal: temporal phrase — subject — verb + infinitive of purpose.
segments.p2-8-g149: Голова is the grammatical subject in nominative. У меня is the idiomatic possessive construction (у + genitive) meaning 'my/at me' (literally 'at me'), so у takes менЯ to show possession. Ещё modifies the verbs: 'still'. Болела and кружилась are imperfective past-tense verbs describing ongoing states ('was aching', 'was spinning'). Со вчерашнего is a shortened genitive phrase meaning 'from yesterday' (со is a variant of с used before certain consonant clusters or for euphony).
segments.p2-8-g150: This is the first half of a correlative comparative construction: Чем ... тем ... ('The more ... the more ...'). Чем + comparative (более) introduces the subordinate clause. Наступал and становились are imperfective past verbs describing progressive change ('was approaching', 'were becoming'). Сумерки is the subject of становились; word order places the temporal notion first and the descriptive clause after.
segments.p2-8-g151: Тем более begins the result clause of the correlative pair and literally means 'so much the more'. Менялись and путались are imperfective past verbs showing increasing change and confusion ('changed', 'became tangled/confused'). Мои впечатления is the plural subject ('my impressions').
segments.p2-8-g152: А за ними is an adjunct meaning 'and after/behind them' or 'following them'; за here with the pronoun in the instrumental marks 'following' or 'along with'. И мысли adds that thoughts (мысли) followed the impressions — a short coordinating clause.
segments.p2-8-g153: Что-то is an indefinite pronoun ('something'); the negative не + past imperfective умирало indicates a continuing refusal to die ('would not die' / 'did not die off'). Во мне внутри is an emphatic double expression meaning 'inside me' (во + pronoun + внутри) with во being the preposition 'in/inside' and внутри reinforcing internal location.
segments.p2-8-g154: В глубине is a locative phrase (в + noun in prepositional or locative form) meaning 'in the depth(s)'. Сердца и совести are genitive singulars (or partitive uses) describing location 'in the depth of the heart and conscience' — a metaphorical internal location.
segments.p2-8-g155: Не хотело умереть repeats the idea with хотеть + infinitive to show unwillingness ('did not want to die'). Сказывалось is an impersonal/patient-form verb meaning 'made itself felt / manifested' (imperfective past), and жгучей тоской is an instrumental noun phrase describing the manner ('by/with a burning longing' — жгучей modifies тоской).
segments.p2-8-g64: "До сих пор" is an adverbial phrase meaning "up to now/so far," followed by the present 1st-person singular reflexive verb "любуюсь" (любоваться) "I admire/admire myself," indicating an ongoing/state action; the inversion (verb before phrase) is stylistic emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g65: "вспоминая" is a gerund (деепричастие) meaning "remembering/recalling," marking a simultaneous action to the main verb; "истинно" is an adverb modifying the adjective that follows, and "джентльменский" is an adjective in the descriptive series that will modify "тон" later.
segments.p2-8-g66: A single adjective in the same descriptive series: "добродушный" (good-natured) continues the list of qualities describing the tone.
segments.p2-8-g67: "открытый тон моего письма" is a noun phrase: "открытый" here means "frank/candid" (an adjectival sense), "тон" = "tone/manner," and "моего письма" is the genitive modifier "of my letter." This whole noun phrase functions as what he is admiring — Dostoevsky uses it as the object of "любуюсь" (stylistically accusative/nominative rather than the expected instrumental).
segments.p2-8-g68: "Ловко и благородно" are adverbial/qualifying words (adverbs) setting the manner in which the action was done: "skillfully and nobly."
segments.p2-8-g69: The particle "а" introduces a mild contrast or continuation in style here ("and/but/while"), functioning as a connective; set off by commas as a parenthetical shift.
segments.p2-8-g70: "главное" is a parenthetical adjective used as an adverbial comment meaning "most importantly" or "above all," focusing what follows.
segments.p2-8-g71: "совершенно без лишних слов" is an adverbial phrase of manner: "completely/absolutely without unnecessary words," where "без" governs the genitive "лишних слов."
segments.p2-8-g72: Main clause: "я обвинил себя во всем." "обвинил" is past tense (perfective) meaning "I blamed/accused," "себя" is the reflexive object, and the prepositional construction "во всем" means "in everything/for everything." Word order (verb before subject) is mildly emphatic.
segments.p2-8-g73: "Оправдывался я" is the past tense reflexive form meaning "I made excuses/I was justifying myself;" the subject "я" follows the verb for stylistic emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g74: Quoted concessive phrase: "если только позволительно мне еще оправдываться" — literally "if only it is permissible to me still to excuse myself." "позволительно" is an impersonal predicate meaning "permissible," and the infinitive "оправдываться" is the action, with "мне" in the dative and "еще" = "still/yet." The whole is parenthetical and self‑deprecating.
segments.p2-8-g75: "тем" (in the set phrase "тем, что") introduces the explanatory clause that follows and can be rendered "by that/that is to say," linking the justification to its reason.
segments.p2-8-g76: "что" is the subordinating conjunction "that," introducing the explanatory content clause.
segments.p2-8-g77: "по совершенной непривычке к вину" is a causal phrase: "по" + noun phrase expresses cause/reason here ("owing to/through"), "совершенной" modifies "непривычке" (= "complete lack of habit"), and "к вину" uses "к" + dative to indicate unfamiliarity "to/with wine."
segments.p2-8-g78: "опьянел" is past tense perfective meaning "became intoxicated"; the phrase "с первой рюмки" uses "с" + genitive to mark source/cause ("from the first small glass/shot").
segments.p2-8-g79: Relative clause modifying "рюмки": "которую (будто бы) выпил еще до них." "которую" agrees with рюмка (fem. acc.), "будто бы" is a parenthetical particle pair meaning "as if/seemingly," and "выпил" is past tense "drank/has drunk;" "еще до них" means "even/before them."
segments.p2-8-g80: "когда поджидал их в Hotel de Paris с пяти до шести часов" is a temporal subordinate clause introduced by "когда" (when). "поджидал" is imperfective past "was waiting/awaiting," "их" = "them," the place is "в Hotel de Paris," and the time interval "с пяти до шести часов" = "from five to six o'clock."
segments.p2-8-g81: Извинения is the plural noun 'apologies' (direct object). Просил is the past tense of просить 'asked/requested.' The word order (object before verb: 'Извинения просил я') places emphasis on what was requested; я is the subject. Преимущественно is an adverb 'predominantly/mostly.' У + name (у Симонова) indicates from/at the person — here 'from Simonov' (the person from whom apologies were requested).
segments.p2-8-g82: Его is the pronoun 'him' (accusative), the object of the implied action 'I asked him to do something.' Же is an emphatic particle adding contrast/emphasis ('him, in particular'). Просил передать is a verb + infinitive construction 'asked [someone] to convey'; передать takes the direct object 'мои объяснения' ('my explanations'), and the whole phrase 'его же просил передать мои объяснения' means 'I asked him as well to pass on my explanations.' И всем другим adds an additional set of recipients ('and to all the others'), where всем is dative plural 'to all.'
segments.p2-8-g83: Особенно is the adverb 'especially' modifying the following dative name, emphasizing that Zverkov was a particular recipient. Зверкову is dative singular of the surname Zverkov, 'to Zverkov.'
segments.p2-8-g84: Которого is the relative pronoun in accusative masculine singular ('whom' / 'that'), referring back to Зверков and introducing the relative clause that follows — it is the object of the verb in that clause.
segments.p2-8-g85: Помнится мне is an impersonal colloquial construction meaning 'I seem to recall' or 'it comes to my recollection'; помнится is the impersonal verb and мне is the dative experiencer 'to me.' In quotes it marks the narrator's tentative memory of the next phrase.
segments.p2-8-g86: Как сквозь сон is an idiomatic phrase 'as if through a dream' (как = 'as/like', сквозь = 'through', сон = 'dream'), describing the hazy, half‑remembered quality of the narrator's memory.
segments.p2-8-g87: Я is the subject pronoun 'I' resuming the main clause after the parenthetical remembered phrase; commas set it off.
segments.p2-8-g88: Кажется is a parenthetical modal verb 'it seems' or 'I think,' softening the assertion that follows — here 'я, кажется, оскорбил' = 'I, it seems, insulted [him].'
segments.p2-8-g89: Оскорбил is the past tense, perfective verb 'insulted' (masculine singular); its object is the relative pronoun которого earlier (whom). The perfective aspect signals a completed action: 'I insulted him.'
segments.p2-8-g156: Толкался is the past imperfective reflexive verb (толкаться) meaning 'to push oneself / jostle' and here describes a habitual or repeated action; the pronoun я follows the verb (inversion) for a colloquial, narrative rhythm. Больше here functions as an adverb meaning 'more / mostly' modifying the verb. По governs the dative (omitted noun follows in the next segment), so самым людным is in the dative plural: самым is the superlative/emphatic form ('most/very') agreeing with людным ('crowded').
segments.p2-8-g157: Улицам is dative plural of улица and is the noun governed by the preceding по; промышленным is an adjective in the dative plural agreeing with улицам and here means 'industrial / commercial' describing the type of streets.
segments.p2-8-g158: По again governs the dative; Мещанским is an adjective-form used as a place-name ('Meshchanskie [streets]') in the dative plural, i.e. 'along the Meshchanskaya (streets)'. The adjective form stands for the proper name of those streets.
segments.p2-8-g159: По + dative again: Садовой is the dative (or locative) form of the street-name Садовая (Sadovaya), so 'along Sadovaya' — the preposition plus dative indicates motion/placement along that street.
segments.p2-8-g160: У takes the genitive and means 'by/near' here; Юсупова сада is a double genitive construction: Юсупова is the genitive form of the surname used attributively with the noun сад, and сада is also in the genitive governed by у — together 'Yusupov's garden', so 'near Yusupov Garden.'
segments.p2-8-g161: Особенно is an adverb for emphasis ('particularly'); любил is the past imperfective of любить and here expresses a habitual liking ('I used to like'); я follows the verb by inversion. Прохаживаться is the reflexive infinitive 'to stroll' governed by любил. По + этим улицам uses the dative plural to say 'along these streets'; в сумерки is a temporal adverbial ('at dusk').
segments.p2-8-g162: Именно когда = 'just/precisely when' introducing the temporal clause; там is the locative adverb 'there'. Густеет is present tense, 3rd person singular of густеть, 'becomes dense / thickens', with толпа as its subject. Всякого прохожего is a genitive attribute ('of all kinds of passersby'), describing the composition of the crowd.
segments.p2-8-g163: Промышленного и ремесленного are adjectives in the same case as люду and describe the types of people (industrial and artisan); люду is an archaic/collective noun for 'people' used here in the genitive to indicate the crowd's composition ('a crowd of industrial and artisan folk').
segments.p2-8-g164: С + instrumental (озабоченными ... лицами) means 'with faces' and озабоченными is a past participle/adj in instrumental plural meaning 'worried'; до злости is a fixed construction with до + genitive meaning 'to the point of anger' (злости = 'anger').
segments.p2-8-g165: Расходящаяся is a present participle (feminine) agreeing with толпа and means 'dispersing' (расходиться, reflexive). По домам uses по + dative plural to indicate direction 'to their homes'; с + genitive (с дневных заработков) marks source 'from the day's earnings', where дневных заработков is genitive plural.
segments.p2-8-g166: Нравилась is the past form of нравиться and is used impersonally with the experiencer мне (dative): literally '... pleased me' → 'I liked'. The verb agrees with the nominative subject эта грошовая суетня; именно adds emphasis ('precisely/just'). Грошовая is an adjective meaning 'paltry/penny‑ante', modifying суетня ('fuss').
segments.p2-8-g167: This short nominative noun phrase is appositive to the previous one: эта (this) + наглая (insolent/brazen) + прозаичность (the quality of being prosaic). It restates what the narrator liked: a bold, prosaic triviality.
segments.p2-8-g168: В этот раз is a temporal phrase 'on this occasion / this time'. Вся эта уличная толкотня is the nominative subject ('all that street jostling'); ещё больше is a comparative adverbial 'even more'. Меня is the accusative experiencer placed before the verb for emphasis, and раздражала is the past tense, feminine singular of раздражать agreeing with the feminine subject толкотня: 'irritated me.'
segments.p2-8-g192: A brief interjection expressing hesitation or thought; functions as a standalone utterance equivalent to English “Hm” or “Hmm.” No inflection or grammar beyond its pragmatic use.
segments.p2-8-g193: Скверно is a predicative adverb meaning that something is bad; уж is an emphatic particle (here ‘indeed/really’); одно то is a nominal phrase meaning ‘one thing (is that)’, introducing the point that follows. Together they set up a clause introduced by что — literally ‘Bad indeed is one thing (that)…’.
segments.p2-8-g194: Что is the subordinating conjunction ‘that’ introducing the content of the bad thing; она is the 3rd-person singular pronoun ‘she’; увидит is the simple future, 3rd-person singular of the perfective увидеть, meaning ‘will see’ or ‘will catch sight of’.
segments.p2-8-g195: Например is a parenthetical adverb ‘for example’ that qualifies the following phrase; it interrupts the flow to signal that what follows is an illustrative instance of what she will see.
segments.p2-8-g196: Как introduces a clause of manner ‘how’; я is the 1st-person subject ‘I’; живу is the present tense, 1st-person singular of жить, here meaning ‘live’ or more idiomatically ‘how I lead my life’. The clause specifies what she will see.
segments.p2-8-g197: Вчера is a temporal adverb ‘yesterday’; я is the subject ‘I’; таким is the instrumental form of такoй used predicatively (‘like that’/‘that sort of way’) and precedes the verb for emphasis; перед + ей (перед ней) is a prepositional phrase ‘before/in the presence of her’; показался is the past tense, masculine singular of показаться and here means ‘came across/appeared (to someone)’ — so the structure means ‘I appeared to her in that way’.
segments.p2-8-g200: Героем is the noun герой in the instrumental singular, used as a predicative complement after показался (показался героем = ‘appeared as a hero’ or ‘came across as a hero’). The standalone sentence fragment emphasizes that role.
segments.p2-8-g177: Мучила is a past-tense verb (imperfective) that agrees with the feminine noun мысль (the hidden subject) and is placed before the object for emphasis: 'Мучила меня' = 'tormented me'. Постоянно is an adverb modifying the verb, and мысль is the delayed nominative subject (a common word-order inversion: Verb + Object + Adverb + Subject).
segments.p2-8-g178: Что introduces a content (subordinate) clause: 'that Liza will come.' Придёт is the simple future, 3rd person singular of прийти; word order here places the verb before the proper name (в English you'd normally say 'that Liza would come' but Russian allows 'что придёт Лиза' for slight emphasis or colloquial tone).
segments.p2-8-g179: Странно functions as an impersonal predicative ('it was strange'); мне is the dative/experiential 'to me' marking the person experiencing the feeling; было is the past tense neuter copular (impersonal construction), and то is a demonstrative pronoun ('that') introducing the following subordinate clause.
segments.p2-8-g180: Что begins the clause. Из всех этих вчерашних воспоминаний is a prepositional phrase ('out of all these yesterday's memories') placed before the subject for contrast. Воспоминание о ней is the nominative subject ('the memory of her'), and как-то особенно is a manner adverbial ('somehow especially') formed by как-то (an adverb meaning 'somewhat/somehow') plus особенно ('especially'); together they modify the verb that follows.
segments.p2-8-g181: Как-то (repeated here) + совсем отдельно is an adverbial cluster intensifying manner: 'in a somewhat, entirely separate way.' Меня is the direct object ('me'), and мучило is past tense neuter singular agreeing with the subject воспоминание (neuter); the verb appears at the end of the clause, as is typical in Russian subordinate clauses.
segments.p2-8-g182: Обо всем другом is a prepositional phrase meaning 'about everything else' (обо required before the vowel), where другом is 'other' modifying всем. Я is the subject, к вечеру is a time expression 'by evening,' and уже совсем успел забыть uses успел + infinitive to mean 'managed (in time) to forget,' with уже совсем adding emphasis ('already quite').
segments.p2-8-g183: Рукой махнул is a short clause of manner + verb: рукой (instrumental) indicates how he acted ('with a hand' = 'waved his hand' or 'dismissed with a wave'), махнул is past tense masculine. И links this to the next clause. Всё ещё совершенно оставался доволен: всё ещё = 'still,' совершенно = 'completely,' оставался is imperfective past 'remained,' and доволен takes an instrumental complement (моим письмом) telling what he was satisfied with; к Симонову specifies the addressee of that letter.
segments.p2-8-g184: Но тут is a discourse marker ('but/at that point') introducing change of mood; я как-то уж не был доволен is a past-tense statement: был (past of быть) + adjective доволен forms a predicative 'was (not) satisfied'. Как-то уж is a colloquial adverbial cluster ('somehow' + intensifier), softening and qualifying the negation не был доволен.
segments.p2-8-g203: 'а' here is a simple coordinating particle used as a discourse connector meaning 'and/well' to introduce what follows; 'теперь' is an adverb 'now' marking the immediate moment or shift in the speaker's thought — the comma shows a brief pause.
segments.p2-8-g204: 'гм' is an interjection (onomatopoeic hesitation sound) transcribed with an exclamation mark to show a thoughtful 'hmm' or momentary pause in speech.
segments.p2-8-g205: 'Это' functions as the demonstrative/dummy subject ('this' or 'it') introducing the comment about the situation; it stands before the parenthetical 'впрочем' and the predicate.
segments.p2-8-g206: 'впрочем' is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'however' or 'still', used to soften or qualify the preceding demonstrative; it interrupts the flow and flags a contrast or reservation.
segments.p2-8-g207: 'скверно' is an evaluative predicate (an archaic/stylistic adjective used adverbially) meaning 'bad/disgraceful' — here it comments on the whole ensuing clause; the comma separates it from the subordinate clause introduced by 'что'.
segments.p2-8-g208: The clause 'что я так опустился' is a subordinate nominal clause introduced by the conjunction 'что' ('that'); 'я' is the subject; 'так' is an intensifying adverb 'so/so much'; 'опустился' is past reflexive meaning 'have sunk/abased myself' (i.e. fallen into a low state) and functions as the main predicate of the subordinate clause.
segments.p2-8-g209: This is a verbless Russian sentence where 'Просто' ('simply') modifies the whole statement, 'нищета' is a nominative noun used as a nominal predicate ('poverty/squalor'), and 'в квартире' is a prepositional phrase ('in the apartment') giving location; the overall meaning is 'It's simply poverty in the apartment.'
segments.p2-8-g210: 'И' is a simple conjunction ('and') adding the remark to the ongoing narrative; 'я решился' is a past reflexive verb meaning 'I dared / summoned the courage' (here with emotional emphasis rather than a mere neutral 'decided'); 'вчера' places the time ('yesterday'). The verb 'решиться' takes the infinitive 'ехать' ('to go/ride') as its complement, and 'ехать ... обедать' expresses motion with the purpose 'to dine' — 'в таком платье' (preposition + prepositional adjective phrase) indicates manner/attire. The exclamation mark underscores the speaker's astonishment or self-reproach.
segments.p2-8-g211: А is a short coordinating particle here with a contrastive feel ('but/and'); клеёнчатый диван is a noun phrase in nominative (adjective + noun) meaning 'oilcloth-covered sofa'. The enclitic particle -то after диван (диван-то) is an emphatic particle that singles out the sofa ('my very sofa' / 'that very sofa'), and мой is the possessive adjective agreeing in gender/number (masculine singular) with диван.
segments.p2-8-g212: из которого is a preposition + relative pronoun combination: из takes the genitive, so которого is the genitive form of который referring back to диван ('out of which / from which'). Мочалка is the nominative subject of торчит; торчит is a present tense, imperfective verb meaning 'protrudes/sticks out' (third person singular), giving the exclamatory clause 'a scrubbing cloth sticks out of it'.
segments.p2-8-g213: А again introduces the remark with a contrastive/continuative tone. Халат (nominative) is 'dressing gown' here; the particle -то after халат (халат-то) emphatically singles out the garment, and мой is the possessive adjective agreeing with халат (masculine singular).
segments.p2-8-g214: Которым is the instrumental (or sometimes ablative) form of который used here with the impersonal modal нельзя plus a reflexive verb: 'которым нельзя закрыться' = 'with which one cannot close/cover oneself.' Нельзя is an impersonal modal meaning 'it is impossible / one cannot / not allowed,' and закрыться is the infinitive reflexive 'to close/cover oneself,' so the clause means that the gown is useless for covering oneself.
segments.p2-8-g215: Какие is an exclamatory form here ('what...!') modifying клочья; клочья is a plural noun meaning 'shreds/tatters.' The short exclamation expresses contempt or disgust at the state of the garments.
segments.p2-8-g218: И is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking this clause to the previous thought. Она is the subject 'she'; это всё is the object phrase 'all this/it all' (это demonstrative + всё 'all'); увидит is future tense, perfective 'will see' (third person singular), so the clause says 'and she will see all this.'
segments.p2-8-g219: и again links the second clause, Аполлона is the accusative singular of Аполлон and here likely used figuratively for a handsome or striking man ('Apollo' as an epithet); увидит repeats the future tense 'will see.' Together: 'and she'll see Apollo as well.'
segments.p2-8-g90: Я прибавлял is past imperfective (прибавлять) in the 1st person singular: literally 'I was adding / I added'. The imperfective past here reports what the narrator said or appended (introducing the following clause with что). Word order (subject before verb) is neutral.
segments.p2-8-g91: что introduces a reported-clause ('that...'). и сам бы combines the particle и (here: emphatic 'even/also') with сам ('myself', emphatic) and the conditional particle бы attached to the past tense поехал to form a hypothetical: 'I myself would have gone.' ко + всем = к (to/toward) with весь in dative plural (всем) = 'to all of them.'
segments.p2-8-g92: да here is a colloquial coordinating particle linking/explaining an excuse ('but/and so'), followed by the nominative subject голова and the present tense verb болит (3rd person sg) meaning 'the head hurts' i.e. 'my head aches.' This is an immediate physical excuse.
segments.p2-8-g93: а contrasts and introduces the strongest reason; пуще всего is an older/comparative phrasing meaning 'more than anything / above all' (пуще = 'more'), and совестно is an impersonal predicate meaning 'ashamed' (elliptical for 'I feel ashamed' — common colloquial construction). The dash emphasizes the final point.
segments.p2-8-g94: Особенно is an adverb 'especially'; доволен is a short-form adjective meaning 'pleased/satisfied' which governs the instrumental case for the thing that pleases (доволен чем). Остался (past of остаться) here means 'remained/was left' — the order 'доволен остался я' is stylistic inversion (predicate before subject). этой 'некоторой легкостью' is feminine instrumental (этой, некоторой, лёгкостью) specifying what pleased him.
segments.p2-8-g95: даже (even) intensifies the next phrase; чуть не is the idiom 'almost' (чуть alone = 'slightly', but чуть не + verb/noun = 'almost'); небрежностию is the feminine instrumental of небрежность with an archaic/dialectal ending -ю (19th-century style), repeating the instrumental that goes with доволен — 'almost carelessness'. Впрочем in the parenthesis signals a qualification 'however'.
segments.p2-8-g96: совершенно is an adverb 'completely/entirely'; приличною is an archaic/dialectal instrumental form (ending -ю) of the adjective приличный, agreeing with the earlier instrumental noun: '(however, entirely respectable).' The parenthesis reverses the negative impression just mentioned.
segments.p2-8-g97: которая is a feminine relative pronoun referring back to 'некоторой легкостью'. вдруг ('suddenly') modifies отразилась (reflexive perfective 'was reflected/was manifested') — here: 'showed itself in my writing.' в моем пере literally 'in my pen' (пере = older spelling/form of перо) indicates the medium. и лучше всех возможных резонов is an adverbial comparative phrase 'and, better than any possible reasons/arguments', where лучше (comparative 'better') governs the genitive plural всех возможных резонов.
segments.p2-8-g98: сразу is an adverb meaning 'immediately' or 'at once', marking the timing of the next action.
segments.p2-8-g99: давала им понять uses давать (imperfective past feminine to agree with которая) + infinitive понять: literally 'gave them to understand' = 'made them understand' or 'conveyed to them'. Им is dative 'to them'. The imperfective verb emphasizes the effect produced rather than a single completed act.
segments.p2-8-g100: что introduces an indirect statement: я смотрю (present 'I regard/consider') + на всю эту вчерашнюю гадость is the prepositional/accusative phrase 'at all that yesterday's filth' (i.e. 'that whole filthy business of yesterday'); довольно независимо is an adverbial phrase 'fairly/quite independently' meaning 'with detachment.' The quoted phrase is treated as an object of смотреть in the sense 'to regard/consider.'
segments.p2-8-g101: совсем-таки is a combined intensifier (совсем 'completely' + particle таки for emphasis) used here as a colloquial intensification before the following negative statement.
segments.p2-8-g102: вовсе-таки reinforces the negation (вовсе 'at all' in negation + таки for emphasis); не убит наповал is a verbless/predicate construction where убит is a past passive participle used predicatively ('killed') negated by не, and наповал ('to the ground/utterly') is an adverb intensifier: 'by no means killed stone-dead.'
segments.p2-8-g103: как вы is a short comparative/addressing phrase 'as you' (i.e. 'as you do/think'). Here it introduces the expected opinion of the addressees.
segments.p2-8-g104: господа is a vocative/plural address 'gentlemen' (colloquial/formal address to the listeners).
segments.p2-8-g105: вероятно is an adverb 'probably' qualifying the following verb думаете.
segments.p2-8-g106: думаете is 2nd person plural present of думать: 'you think' (addressing the gentlemen). The sentence thus reports what the listeners probably think.
segments.p2-8-g107: а напротив is a two-word contrast: а ('but/on the other hand') + напротив ('on the contrary'), signaling that the narrator's actual stance is opposite to what they suppose.
segments.p2-8-g108: смотрю так = 'I look/consider thus' — смотрю (1st sg present) here means 'I regard' and так 'so/thus' points to the manner in which he regards the matter.
segments.p2-8-g109: как следует смотреть на это спокойно уважающему себя джентльмену is a comparative clause: как следует (idiomatically 'as is proper/one ought to') + смотреть на это ('to look at / regard this'), followed by the dative phrase спокойно уважающему себя джентльмену — 'to a self-respecting gentleman who is calm' (уважающему is a present active participle in the dative, себя = 'himself', джентльмену dative). The whole means 'the way a calm, self-respecting gentleman ought to look at it.'
segments.p2-8-g110: Быль is a noun meaning 'a true story' or 'something that really happened' used here as the clause's subject; stylistically emphatic.
segments.p2-8-g111: дескать is a quotative/particle meaning 'they say' or 'so they say' (often used ironically), marking the reported or folkloric tone.
segments.p2-8-g112: молодцу (dative) + не укор (nominative noun with negation) is an elliptic construction meaning 'it is no reproach to the fellow' or 'one cannot reproach the fellow for that.' Russian often omits a copula here; the sense is 'to a fine fellow it is not a fault.'
segments.p2-8-g113: This whole line is an interrogative, with even/emphatic particles: Даже ('even/indeed') + ведь ('after all/you know') set the tone; какая-то is the indefinite expression 'some kind of' (какая 'what/such' + -то particle to make it indefinite); игривость is a feminine noun 'playfulness'; маркизская is an attributive adjective 'marquis's / marquis-like' agreeing in feminine nominative with игривость. The speaker questions whether the attitude has an aristocratic, marquis-like playfulness.
segments.p2-8-g220: Эта is the feminine demonstrative 'this' (agreeing with the feminine noun скотина). Скотина is a derogatory noun here meaning a contemptuous person; the noun phrase functions as the sentence subject.
segments.p2-8-g221: Наверно is a colloquial short form of наверное and is used parenthetically as an adverb of probability ('probably'). It simply qualifies the following predicate.
segments.p2-8-g222: Оскорбит is a third-person singular future (perfective) form of оскорбить: 'will insult'. Ее is the accusative pronoun 'her' placed before the verb (common, neutral word order). Together they form the verbal predicate 'will insult her.'
segments.p2-8-g223: Он is the pronoun 'he' (subject). Придерется is third-person singular future of придираться/придраться with the reflexive ending -ся; here it means 'will pick on/find fault with.' К + ней is the prepositional phrase 'at/to her' (к governs the dative form after preposition), showing the target of the nitpicking.
segments.p2-8-g224: Чтоб introduces a purpose clause ('so that' / 'in order that'). Мне is the dative 'to me' indicating the person affected. Сделать is the infinitive 'to do/make' and грубость is the noun 'a rudeness' — together 'чтоб мне сделать грубость' = 'in order to do me a rudeness' (i.e., to be rude to me).
segments.p2-8-g225: А is a coordinating conjunction often translated 'and' or 'but' setting up contrast; я is the subject 'I'. Уж here is an emphatic/colloquial particle ('indeed' / emphatic marker) stressing the speaker's resignation before the following comment.
segments.p2-8-g226: Разумеется is an adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally'; it is parenthetical and reinforces the expectedness of the behavior that follows.
segments.p2-8-g227: По + обычаю is a prepositional phrase meaning 'according to custom' or 'as usual'; по governs the dative/locative sense here and обычаю (dative/singular form of обычай) means 'custom/practice.'
segments.p2-8-g228: Струшу is first-person singular future (colloquial) meaning 'I will chicken out' or 'I'll back down'; it is a single-word future predicate that lists one expected reaction.
segments.p2-8-g229: An infinitive (семенить 'to shuffle/scurry') appears before the auxiliary-like verb начну ('I will begin'), a common Russian pattern where the infinitive expresses the action that will be started. Перед ней is a prepositional phrase 'in front of her / before her (in her presence)' showing the location/target.
segments.p2-8-g230: Закрываться is the imperfective infinitive or reflexive verb 'to cover/close oneself (up)'; полами халата is an instrumental phrase literally 'with the skirts (полы) of the robe (халат)', describing the means ('wrapping myself with the skirts of my robe'). The whole phrase is another nominal infinitive phrase functioning as the object of начну (implied).
segments.p2-8-g231: Улыбаться is the infinitive 'to smile' placed before начну to indicate the action the speaker will begin; начну (implied here as in repeated pattern) is 'I will begin.' This is a serial listing of behaviours the speaker expects to start.
segments.p2-8-g232: Лгать is the infinitive 'to lie' (tell lies), again presented with начну (I will begin) to complete the series of actions the speaker predicts he will undertake.
segments.p2-8-g233: У here functions as an interjection/exclamation (colloquial 'ugh' or 'oh'); grammatically it is an exclamatory particle rather than its usual prepositional uses.
segments.p2-8-g234: Скверность is a noun used exclamatorily meaning 'nasty/vileness' or 'meanness'; here it expresses the speaker's disgust at the whole situation.
segments.p2-8-g235: Да и: colloquial introductory phrase (Да + и) here means 'and/besides' or 'moreover' and adds emphasis. Не в этом: a negative locative phrase 'not in this' (в + demonstrative этим) functioning adverbially to indicate where the fault is not. Главная-то скверность: a nominal predicate with the emphatic particle -то attached to главная, highlighting 'the main badness/evil' — so the whole clause reads literally 'The main badness is not in this,' with the particles giving colloquial force.
segments.p2-8-g236: Тут есть: an existential construction ('there is' / 'here is') with тут (here) + есть (to be/exist). Что-то: indefinite pronoun 'something' (что + -то). Главнее: comparative adjective 'more main' = 'more important' used as the complement/predicate of the existential phrase.
segments.p2-8-g237: Гаже: comparative/adverbial adjective meaning 'worse' or 'much worse' — used here as an exclamatory brief comparative to intensify the idea just stated.
segments.p2-8-g238: Подлее!: comparative of подлый, 'more base' or 'meaner' — another exclamatory comparative piled after гаже for stronger moral condemnation.
segments.p2-8-g239: Да,: a short confirming interjection 'yes' or 'indeed' used to endorse or repeat the previous claim.
segments.p2-8-g240: Подлее!: repeated exclamatory comparative (see above), standing alone as an emphatic one-word sentence meaning 'meaner' or 'more base.'
segments.p2-8-g241: И опять,: conjunction 'and' plus adverb 'again' used as an initial connective/introductory phrase; the comma plus repetition of 'опять' in the next segment intensifies the sense of repetition or inevitability.
segments.p2-8-g242: Опять надевать: opyat' + imperfective infinitive nadyevat' ('to put on, to don') — repetition + infinitive expresses a habitual or recurring action (here exclamatory: 'to have to put on again'). Эту бесчестную лживую маску: direct object in accusative (эту + adjectives agreeing in gender/case/number) — 'this dishonorable, lying mask' where маску is used figuratively as 'guise/veneer.'
segments.p2-8-g245: Дойдя is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) of дойти meaning "having reached"; до governs the genitive (до этой мысли = "to/ up to this thought"). The whole phrase sets a prior, completed action that leads into the main clause.
segments.p2-8-g246: Я — subject in the nominative; вспыхнул is past tense, perfective, 1st person singular of вспыхнуть meaning an emotional "flared up". Так и is an adverbial particle combination here (так = "so/thus", и used as an intensifier meaning roughly "indeed/just"), giving force or immediacy to the reaction.
segments.p2-8-g247: Для чего is an interrogative phrase meaning "for what" / "why" (literally "for what reason"); бесчестную is the feminine adjective in the accusative (used elliptically as if naming someone or calling her "dishonorable"). The clause is elliptical and rhetorical — the full verb/object is omitted but understood from context.
segments.p2-8-g248: Какую is the interrogative pronoun (accusative feminine singular) asking "which/what kind of"; бесчестную repeats the adjective (again used elliptically as a nominal phrase). Together they challenge or request clarification of the preceding label.
segments.p2-8-g249: Я is the first-person singular subject; говорил is the imperfective past tense (masculine) of говорить, here simply "I spoke/said"; вчера is a temporal adverb "yesterday"; искренно is an adverb modifying говорил, meaning "sincerely." The word order puts time before manner but all are normal adverbial complements to the past verb.
segments.p2-8-g250: Я помню is present tense, 1st person singular of помнить — "I remember." It introduces or stresses the speaker's memory as a frame for the following statement.
segments.p2-8-g251: Во мне is the prepositional expression "in me" (во used instead of в for euphony before the pronoun); тоже means "also/too"; было is past neuter used existentially here ("there was"); настоящее modifies чувство (neuter) and means "genuine/real feeling." Together: "there was also a genuine feeling in me."
segments.p2-8-g299: 'непременно' is an adverb of certainty modifying the verb; 'придет' is the perfective future, 3rd person singular of 'прийти' (to come), with an omitted subject (impersonal/unspecified 'it/he/that') and an exclamatory force.
segments.p2-8-g300: 'восклицал' is the past imperfective, masculine singular form meaning 'I exclaimed' (recounting a speaking action); the dash marks reported speech and 'я' is the explicit 1st-person subject.
segments.p2-8-g301: 'бегая' is a present gerund (деепричастие) from 'бегать', indicating a simultaneous or accompanying action ('running/about') and describing how the speaker exclaimed; 'по комнате' uses 'по' with the prepositional 'комнате' to mean 'around/in the room'.
segments.p2-8-g302: Simple negation phrase: 'не' negates the adverbial time word 'сегодня' ('not today'); it's an elliptical part of the reported sentence ('not today').
segments.p2-8-g303: 'так' here functions as a connective/adverb ('then/in that case'); 'завтра' is an adverb of time ('tomorrow'); 'придет' is again the perfective future, 3rd person singular ('will come'), forming the clause 'then tomorrow it will come.'
segments.p2-8-g304: 'а' is a contrastive conjunction ('and/but'); 'уж' is an emphatic particle intensifying certainty ('indeed/surely'); 'отыщет' is the perfective future, 3rd person singular of 'отыскать' ('will find'), giving the force 'and it will surely find (it).' The phrase is forceful and colloquial.
segments.p2-8-g305: 'И' is a simple conjunction ('and'); 'таков' is a predicative adjective meaning 'such is' or 'such is the case'; the noun phrase 'проклятый романтизм' ('damned romanticism') is the subject/predicate complement, and 'всех этих чистых сердец' is a genitive plural attribute meaning 'of all these pure hearts' (the romanticism belongs to them).
segments.p2-8-g306: Here 'О' functions as an exclamatory particle ('Oh!') rather than the preposition 'about'; 'мерзость' is a strong noun meaning 'abomination' or 'loathsomeness' used as an exclamatory noun.
segments.p2-8-g307: Repeated exclamatory 'о' ('Oh!') followed by 'глупость' ('stupidity' or 'folly'), another noun used in exclamation to condemn the thing named.
segments.p2-8-g308: Again exclamatory 'о' + noun phrase: 'ограниченность' ('narrowness, limitedness') is the head, and 'этих поганых сантиментальных душ' is a genitive plural attribute meaning 'of these filthy/sordid sentimental souls'; the adjectives 'поганых' and 'сантиментальных' modify 'душ' ('souls').
segments.p2-8-g285: Two coordinated past imperfective verbs (сознавал, боялся) describe ongoing or habitual states in the past. The noun phrase эту мою слабую струнку follows the normal Russian demonstrative–possessive–adjective–noun order for emphasis, and the pronoun ее refers back to струнку as a direct object. The colon introduces the quoted self-remark that the narrator repeats.
segments.p2-8-g286: This is the beginning of the quoted clause. Вcе-то is an emphatic construction: всё (everything) plus the particle-то marks the cause/point being stressed; я преувеличиваю is present imperfective, first person singular, confessing a habitual trait. The phrase functions as the narrator's self-accusation inside quotation marks.
segments.p2-8-g287: Тем is the instrumental/demonstrative form used adverbially here ('by that' / 'for that reason'); и is a simple connective/intensifier; хромаю is present tense, first person singular of хромать ('to limp'/'to be defective'), used figuratively to mean 'that's my failing.' The clause completes the quoted self-judgment.
segments.p2-8-g288: Повторял is past imperfective (habitual repetition) and the word order 'повторял я себе' stresses the speaker and the reflexive dative себе ('to myself'). Ежечасно is an adverb meaning 'hourly' or 'constantly', emphasizing frequent repetition. The dash before the verb marks the end of the quotation and resumes the narrator's voice.
segments.p2-8-g289: Но is a coordinating conjunction introducing a contrast or slight correction; here it opens the narrator's comment. It is a common sentence-initial connective and does not change the verb forms that follow.
segments.p2-8-g290: Впрочем as an introductory particle softens or qualifies what precedes (equivalent to 'however' or 'in any case'); it is set off by commas when sentence-initial. It repeats the hedging sense used immediately after.
segments.p2-8-g291: The same particle впрочем appears again inside the quoted refrain; inside quotation marks it functions as the speaker's hedging word ('well'/'anyway') and is part of the quoted, hesitant utterance.
segments.p2-8-g292: Всё-таки is an adverbial phrase (here split into two segments) meaning 'after all' or 'still' and expresses reluctance or concession; Лиза is the proper name and serves as the (subject) of the quoted clause. Word order puts the hedging adverb before the name for natural spoken emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g293: Пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'probably' or 'perhaps' that hedges the certainty of the prediction; it modifies the following verb and is typical in spoken, tentative assertions.
segments.p2-8-g294: Придет is the future tense, third-person singular of прийти ('will come'); here it closes the quoted hopeful prediction. Вот припев uses вот as a demonstrative ('this is') and припев ('refrain') to label the quoted sentence as the recurring phrase that frames the narrator's thoughts.
segments.p2-8-g295: Которым is a relative pronoun in the instrumental used adverbially ('by which' / 'in which'); заключались (impersonal/passive-style imperfective past) here means 'consisted' or 'were summed up.' The phrase literally reads 'in which all my then-reasonings consisted,' with все мои тогдашние рассуждения as the subject of заключались.
segments.p2-8-g296: До того is an adverbial phrase meaning 'to such an extent' or 'so much' (literally 'up to that'), followed by the past imperfective беспокоился describing an ongoing state of worry. The construction stresses degree: 'I worried to that point.'
segments.p2-8-g297: The subordinate clause introduced by что gives the result: приходил (past imperfective of приходить) here has the sense 'would come/occur' in the sense 'would come upon me,' and в бешенство is an idiom meaning 'into a fury' or 'into a rage.' Иногда modifies приходил: 'would sometimes come into a rage.'
segments.p2-8-g298: Придет is third-person singular future of прийти and here appears as a standalone exclamation in quotation marks; its use and punctuation indicate a brief confident inner or spoken prediction. The future tense expresses expectation rather than habitual action.
segments.p2-8-g270: Именно (emphatic adverb) stresses that it was this one particular item; 'один момент из всего вчерашнего' is a noun phrase in nominative meaning 'one moment out of all of yesterday'. 'мне' is a dative experiencer, and 'представлялся' is the past tense, imperfective reflexive/intransitive verb meaning 'presented itself/appeared' — together: 'one moment … presented itself to me'.
segments.p2-8-g271: 'это' refers back to the singled-out moment; 'когда' introduces the temporal clause 'when'. 'я' is the subject; 'осветил' is perfective past 'lit' (action completed). 'спичкой' is the instrumental tool ('with a match'), 'комнату' is the direct object in accusative. After 'и' the verb 'увидал' is past perfective 'saw' with 'ее' (her) as the object and 'бледное' beginning the adjectival description of what was seen.
segments.p2-8-g272: 'искривленное' is an adjectival past participle / adjective modifying 'лицо' (neuter noun). Together 'бледное, искривленное лицо' is a coordinated adjectival phrase describing the face that he saw.
segments.p2-8-g273: 'с' + instrumental 'мученическим взглядом' means 'with a martyr-like/tormented gaze'; the adjective 'мученическим' qualifies 'взглядом' (instrumental), giving the manner of the look on the face.
segments.p2-8-g274: И is a connective/exclamatory particle here; 'какая' is the exclamatory interrogative used as 'what a…' and 'жалкая' is an adjective 'pitiable' describing the smile that follows — the fragment is an exclamatory preface to the full noun phrase.
segments.p2-8-g275: Another exclamatory 'какая' continues the piling up of adjectives; 'неестественная' describes the smile as 'unnatural' or 'affected' — the sequence intensifies the narrator's emotional judgment.
segments.p2-8-g276: 'какая' again introduces the final adjective phrase; 'искривленная улыбка' is the noun phrase 'distorted smile'. 'у ней была' is a construction expressing possession/appearance ('she had' / 'was on her'): 'у' + pronoun in dative (here 'ней') plus past 'была'. 'в ту минуту' is a temporal adverbial 'at that moment'.
segments.p2-8-g277: Simple past clause: 'Но' = 'but'; 'я' subject; 'еще не знал' = 'did not yet know' (imperfective past, meaning he was not aware at that time); 'тогда' = 'then' (temporal adverb).
segments.p2-8-g278: 'что' introduces the content clause. 'и' here has the sense 'even'/'also' (emphatic): 'и через пятнадцать лет' = 'even in/after fifteen years'. 'через пятнадцать лет' is a time expression 'in/after fifteen years'. 'я' is subject; 'всё-таки' (split here into 'все' + 'таки') is an adverbial phrase meaning 'all the same'/'after all'; 'буду представлять себе' is future imperfective 'I will imagine (to myself)'; 'Лизу' is the object in accusative; 'именно' = 'exactly/precisely'; 'с этой жалкой' begins the prepositional phrase 'with that pitiable…' describing how he will imagine her.
segments.p2-8-g279: 'искривленной' continues the coordinated adjectives modifying 'улыбкой' and is an adjectival participle meaning 'distorted'/'crooked'. Its case (instrumental) matches 'улыбкой' since the whole phrase is 'с этой жалкой, искривленной… улыбкой'.
segments.p2-8-g280: 'ненужной' ('unnecessary'/'unwanted') is the final adjective in the string, and 'улыбкой' is instrumental singular of 'улыбка' — the whole phrase 'с этой … улыбкой' is an instrumental complement indicating the manner in which he will imagine Liza.
segments.p2-8-g281: Relative clause 'которая у ней была в ту минуту' modifies 'улыбкой'. 'которая' = 'which/that' (feminine, agreeing with 'улыбка'), 'у' + 'ней' expresses possession/appearance ('she had'), 'была' past 'was', and 'в ту минуту' locates the original occurrence in time ('at that moment').
segments.p2-8-g282: На другой день is a temporal phrase 'on the next day'; я уже опять готов был счeтать uses the past-periphrastic construction был готов + infinitive считать = 'was ready/willing to consider'. Все это is the object ('all this'), and вздором is in the instrumental case because считать + accusative + instrumental (считать X чем) requires an instrumental predicate: 'to consider X to be Y'.
segments.p2-8-g283: Развозившимися нервами is an instrumental plural participial phrase (развозившимися = active participle with reflexive -ся, instrumental plural) functioning as another complement to считать: 'as (caused by) nerves being spread/wound up.' The instrumental marks the role/characterization (similar to 'as [a result of]...').
segments.p2-8-g284: А главное is a contrastive/contrast-adding phrase 'and/above all'; преувеличением is again instrumental, completing считать: 'and, above all, an exaggeration.' The dash merely marks emphasis before the final complement.
segments.p2-8-g252: The subject Я is the first-person pronoun. Именно is an adverb of emphasis meaning “specifically/precisely,” modifying the following predicate. Хотел is the past-tense (masculine) form of хотеть expressing an intention or desire; it takes the infinitive вызвать (“to cause/evoke”) as its complement.
