В ней is the prepositional phrase meaning “in her” (locative, indicating feelings occurring within her), and благородные чувства is the direct object phrase (‘noble feelings’), with благородные as an adjectival modifier and чувства in the plural.
segments.p2-8-g255: Если is the conditional conjunction “if.” Она is the subject “she.” Поплакала is the past-tense, perfective form of поплакать meaning “cried a little” or “shed some tears”; as perfective past it presents the action as a completed, short act (here in a hypothetical/possible sense).
segments.p2-8-g256: То is the particle that pairs with если to mean “then” in a conditional clause; это is the demonstrative pronoun “this/that” referring to the fact just mentioned; хорошо is an adverb meaning “good” or “well” assessing that fact. Together: “then that is good.”
segments.p2-8-g257: Это again refers to the foregoing situation (“that/this”), благотворно is an adverb meaning “beneficially,” and подействует is the future tense, 3rd person singular of the perfective verb подействовать meaning “to have an effect / to act” — here “will have an effect.”
segments.p2-8-g260: Но is the adversative conjunction “but/yet.” Все-таки (here split into two words in the data) is a concessive particle meaning “after all / all the same / nonetheless” that softens or concedes the preceding point. Я is the subject “I.” Никак is an intensifying adverb combining with the negation не to mean “in no way / by no means,” and не is the verbal negation. Мог is the past-tense form of мочь (“could / was able to”), and успокоиться is the reflexive infinitive “to calm down” (the reflexive ending indicates the subject calming himself). The phrase никак не мог успокоиться thus means “could not calm down at all” (strong inability).
segments.p2-8-g261: Весь is the determiner “whole/entire” modifying этот вечер (“this evening”); together весь этот вечер = “the whole/entire evening.” Word order is a normal noun phrase headed by the noun вечер.
segments.p2-8-g262: Уже means “already” (temporal), когда is the temporal conjunction “when.” Я is the subject “I.” И here has the sense “even”/“also,” adding emphasis (я и домой воротился = “I even returned home”/“I had even gone back home”). Домой is the directional adverb “home,” and воротился is the past-tense of воротиться (= вернуться), “returned” (colloquial/older usage). The clause gives a temporal frame “already when I had even returned home.”
segments.p2-8-g263: Уже again “already,” после is the preposition “after” marking time, and девяти часов is the genitive time expression “nine o’clock” (lit. “of nine hours”). The phrase means “already after nine o’clock.”
segments.p2-8-g264: Когда is the temporal conjunction “when,” introducing the subordinate clause that follows; it ties this time phrase to the expectation about Liza’s arrival.
segments.p2-8-g265: По here governs the dative and means “according to / by,” and расчету (dative of расчёт) means “calculation/plan/estimate”; по расчёту = “by calculation / according to the plan/expectation,” i.e. “by all calculations/expectation.”
segments.p2-8-g266: Никак ... не могла is a strong negation construction meaning “could not in any way / could not possibly” (могла is the past-tense, feminine form of мочь). Прийти is the infinitive “to come” (perfective here: to arrive), and Лиза is the subject (proper name). The word order places the verb phrase before the subject for emphasis: “could not possibly have come Liza.”
segments.p2-8-g267: Мне is the dative pronoun “to me,” marking the experiencer (“to me she appeared”). Всё-таки again supplies the concessive sense “still/none the less” (split here into two tokens). Она is the subject “she,” мерещилась is the imperfective reflexive/psychological verb meaning “appeared (to me) / I imagined seeing her” (implying it was an illusion or mental image), and и is the coordinating conjunction “and,” linking to the next clause.
segments.p2-8-g268: Главное is the neuter adjective used nominally/adverbially to mean “the main thing / above all / chiefly”; it introduces the chief point to follow.
segments.p2-8-g269: Вспоминалась is the reflexive verb meaning “was recalled / came to mind” (i.e. she kept coming to mind). Всё here functions as an intensifier meaning something like “constantly/all the time” in this context. В (preposition) + одном и том же положении is the fixed phrase “in one and the same position/situation” (одном = “one/a certain,” и том же combines demonstratives with же as an emphatic particle). Together: “(she) was remembered always in one and the same position.”
segments.p2-8-g417: 'Затем' is an adverb meaning 'then/afterwards' and sets a sequence. 'мы' is the 1st person plural subject 'we'. 'начинаем' is present tense, 1st person plural of начинать (imperfective), used with an infinitive to mean 'begin to (do)'. 'жить-поживать' is a colloquial hyphenated verb pair (infinitive) where the second element shades the meaning (to fare/live along); together they mean roughly 'to live/settle down, live comfortably' and are governed by начинаем.
segments.p2-8-g418: 'едем' is the 1st person plural present of ехать and means 'we go/travel' (literally by vehicle but commonly 'we go/leave'). 'за границу' is a prepositional phrase with за + accusative indicating movement 'to/over the border' = 'abroad'. 'и' is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking to the trailing abbreviation 'т.'.
segments.p2-8-g419: The single letter 'д.' here is an abbreviation completing the common phrase 'и т. д.' (и так далее, 'and so on' / 'etc.'). It is written alone here as the continuation of the abbreviation split across the text.
segments.p2-8-g420: This segment is just a comma punctuation mark separating clauses; it has no lexical content.
segments.p2-8-g421: 'и' is the conjunction 'and'. 'т.' is the abbreviated part of 'и т. д.' (и так далее); here it functions as 'so on' but the single-letter abbreviation is non-lexical and is completed by the following 'д.' in the next segment.
segments.p2-8-g422: Again 'д.' is the final part of the abbreviation 'и т. д.' (и так далее). The closing quote mark is printed immediately after it in the original; together they render as 'etc.' inside the quotation.
segments.p2-8-g346: Особенно is an adverb meaning 'especially' modifying the whole clause. Ободрялся and разгуливался are past-tense, imperfective, reflexive/reciprocal verbs (with -ся) describing repeated or habitual states: 'I became encouraged' and 'I strolled/paced about'. The subject pronoun я comes after the verbs for stylistic inversion (verb-first order is common in Russian to foreground the action). После + часов uses the genitive plural (после девяти часов = 'after nine o'clock').
segments.p2-8-g347: Даже ('even') intensifies начинал ('began'), here in the imperfective past (начинал) indicating he started to do something repeatedly or habitually. Иногда ('sometimes') qualifies мечтать ('to dream/daydream'); мечтать is an imperfective infinitive of mental action. Довольно сладко is an adverbial phrase ('quite/pretty sweetly') modifying the manner of the dreaming. The colon marks reported inner speech or a quotation that follows.
segments.p2-8-g348: The opening of the quoted thought; Я is the first-person pronoun in the nominative introducing the content of the quotation. The comma signals the quoted clause continues.
segments.p2-8-g349: Например is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'for example / for instance', marking the following as an illustrative case.
segments.p2-8-g350: Спасаю is present-tense, first-person singular, imperfective aspect ('I save / I am saving') used here to state a general or repeated psychological conviction rather than a single completed act. Лизу is the accusative form of the proper name Liza (object of спасаю); the supplied sense list did not include the proper-name sense, so it is not matched there.
segments.p2-8-g351: Именно тем is a fixed emphatic phrase: именно = 'precisely/exactly', тем is the instrumental/demonstrative use meaning 'by that / by the very fact', preparing the explanation introduced by что. Together they mean 'precisely by the fact that...'.
segments.p2-8-g352: Что introduces the subordinate clause ('that' / 'that...'). Она is the subject 'she'; ко мне is a dative-direction expression meaning 'to my place / to me' — ко is the variant of к used before the pronoun for euphony; ходит is present-tense imperfective meaning habitual motion here ('she comes/visits (regularly)').
segments.p2-8-g353: А is a contrastive conjunction here ('and/but/while') linking the clauses; я is the nominative subject, ей is the dative pronoun 'to her', and говорю is present-tense 'I say / I tell' — a simple present that reports habitual or immediate speech in the quote.
segments.p2-8-g356: Я is the nominative subject 'I'. Ее here is the direct object 'her' (identical in form to the possessive, but context shows accusative object). Развиваю is first-person present imperfective 'I develop' (in the sense of fostering growth or intellect).
segments.p2-8-g357: Образовываю is a second present-tense verb in a paratactic pair with развиваю; it means 'I form / I educate / I cultivate' (here 'I educate her'). The repetition of two similar verbs gives a rhetorical, accumulative effect.
segments.p2-8-g358: Я is the nominative subject 'I'. The comma sets off the adverb that follows for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g359: Наконец is an adverb meaning 'finally' or 'at last', indicating the culmination of a process and modifying the present-tense verb that follows.
segments.p2-8-g360: Замечаю is first-person singular present 'I notice / I perceive' — a present imperfective verb stating current realization.
segments.p2-8-g361: Что introduces the content clause 'that she loves me'; она is 'she' (subject), меня is the accusative 'me' (object), and любит is present-tense 'loves' — standard subject‑verb‑object order within the subordinate clause.
segments.p2-8-g362: Страстно is an adverb 'passionately' modifying the repeated verb любит. The second repetition of любит strengthens and specifies the intensity: 'loves me, loves me passionately.'
segments.p2-8-g363: Я is the subject. Прикидываюсь is a reflexive verb in the present 'I pretend / I feign' (прикидываться) indicating a deliberate pose or pretense; the -ся marks the reflexive/ middle voice.
segments.p2-8-g364: Что introduces the content of the pretense: 'that I do not understand.' Не is the negation particle; понимаю is present-tense 'I understand'; the parenthetical continues with another negated verb не знаю ('I don't know'), which here admits that he really doesn't know the reason for his pretense. The parenthesis marks an aside.
segments.p2-8-g365: Впрочем is a modal/adversative particle meaning 'however / moreover / anyway' that qualifies the previous clause and signals a slight correction or reservation.
segments.p2-8-g366: Для чего is a frozen interrogative phrase meaning 'for what (reason) / why' (для + interrogative чего). Прикидываюсь repeats the verb 'I pretend', here in the main clause of the parenthesis: 'I don't know, however, for what reason I pretend.'
segments.p2-8-g367: Так is a discourse word 'so / thus / like that' offering a concluding conjecture about motive; it's free-standing and often signals a summary or example.
segments.p2-8-g368: Для красы literally 'for the beauty' — an idiomatic older/poetic phrase meaning 'for the sake of appearances' or 'for ornament/appearance'. Для is 'for / for the sake of' and красы is the genitive of краса used here in a slightly archaic register.
segments.p2-8-g369: Вероятно is an adverb 'probably / apparently' hedging the conjecture just made; the closing parenthesis ends the aside.
segments.p2-8-g309: Ну is an introductory particle here, equivalent to English “well” or “now,” used to start or soften the utterance.
segments.p2-8-g310: Как here means “how” in an exclamatory/rhetorical question; не понять is the infinitive with negation meaning “not to understand” (i.e. “how could one not understand?”).
segments.p2-8-g311: Как бы is a small phrase meaning “as if”/“sort of”; как = “as/like,” and бы is the conditional/modal particle that softens or makes hypothetical the following sense.
segments.p2-8-g312: Кажется is an impersonal verb meaning “it seems” or “apparently,” used here parenthetically to mark the narrator’s uncertainty or impression.
segments.p2-8-g313: Не понять? repeats the infinitive with negation as a trailing rhetorical question — again “not to understand?” — echoing the earlier clause for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g316: This whole clause is a simple past-description: Но = “but,” тут = “at this point / here,” я сам places emphasis on the subject (“I myself”), останавливался is the imperfective past (“paused/kept stopping” or simply “paused”), and в большом смущении is a prepositional phrase meaning “in great embarrassment.”
segments.p2-8-g317: И как мало is an exclamatory opening: И = “and” linking the thought to what came before, как = “how,” and мало = “little / so few” used to introduce the exclamation.
segments.p2-8-g318: The repeated мало is simple emphatic repetition (“little, little” / “so few, so few”) reinforcing the exclamation.
segments.p2-8-g319: Думал я мимоходом is a parenthetical reporting clause: думал is the imperfect past “I thought,” with subject я placed after the verb for stylistic effect, and мимоходом is an adverb meaning “in passing / incidentally.”
segments.p2-8-g320: Нужно было слов is an impersonal construction meaning literally “words were needed” or “it took words”; нужно is a predicative adjective meaning “necessary,” было is past “was,” and слов is genitive plural of слово (quantity: “words”).
segments.p2-8-g321: This clause repeats the same idea focused on the idyll: как мало (how few) нужно было (were needed) идиллии (dative singular: “for the idyll”); да и ... то ... ещё adds a parenthetical qualification—да и = “and/besides,” the particle то attached to идиллии (идиллии-то) is emphatic, ещё = “still,” and напускной = “affected/artificial.”
segments.p2-8-g322: Книжной is an adjectival descriptor meaning “bookish,” coordinated with напускной to qualify the idyll as literary/artificial.
segments.p2-8-g323: Сочиненной is a past passive participle (“composed / invented / contrived”) agreeing with идиллии (feminine), describing the idyll as contrived rather than genuine.
segments.p2-8-g324: Чтоб introduces a purpose/result clause meaning “so that / in order that”; тотчас же = “immediately / at once” (же is emphatic), и here is connective “and/also,” повернуть is a perfective infinitive “to turn” (transitive), всю человеческую душу is the direct object “the whole human soul,” and по-своему is an adverbial phrase meaning “in one’s own way” (по + dative).
segments.p2-8-g325: То-то ... -то is a colloquial/exclamatory pattern for emphasis: the first То is a demonstrative “that,” the attached to the noun and the final то are emphatic particles; девственность here is used metaphorically (“virginity/innocence/purity”).
segments.p2-8-g424: 'Одним словом' is an idiomatic introductory phrase meaning 'in a word' or 'in short'; grammatically it uses the instrumental singular of 'слово' (одним словом) used adverbially to summarize what was said.
segments.p2-8-g425: This is an impersonal construction with dative emphasis: 'самому' (to myself) adds emphasis that the feeling affected the speaker personally; 'подло' is an adverb ('meanly, basely'); 'становилось' is the imperfective past (impersonal or experiential) meaning 'became / began to feel' — literally 'it was becoming (to me) base'.
segments.p2-8-g426: 'и' is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; 'я' is the subject 'I'; 'кончал' is the imperfective past of 'кончать' here used idiomatically 'I would end (up)'; 'тем' is an instrumental demonstrative/pronoun meaning 'by that / with that' and links to the following clause introduced by 'что'.
segments.p2-8-g427: 'что' introduces the content clause ('that/which'); 'дразнил' is the past imperfective of 'дразнить' meaning 'teased' (habitual or repeated action); 'себя' is the reflexive accusative 'myself'; 'языком' is the instrumental of 'язык' ('with the tongue'), indicating the means by which he teased himself.
segments.p2-8-g428: 'Да и' together here functions as a colloquial discourse particle meaning 'and besides / anyway'; 'не' negates the verb; 'пустят' is future/simple present plural of 'пустить' meaning 'they will let / allow'; 'ее' is the accusative object pronoun 'her' (they won't let her).
segments.p2-8-g429: A vocative-like exclamation naming the woman insultingly; 'мерзавку' is the accusative of 'мерзавка' used here as a derogatory label in direct speech.
segments.p2-8-g430: Simple past narrative comment: 'думал' is past tense of 'думать' ('I thought'), with 'я' after the verb for emphasis/colloquial word order (verb before pronoun is normal in Russian past narration).
segments.p2-8-g431: 'Их' is the accusative/plural pronoun 'them' (referring to a group); 'ведь' is a discourse particle meaning roughly 'after all / you know', adding explanatory or confirmatory tone to the remark.
segments.p2-8-g432: 'кажется' is an impersonal present of 'казаться' meaning 'it seems' or 'apparently', signaling the speaker's uncertainty about the statement that follows.
segments.p2-8-g433: 'гулять' is the infinitive 'to go for a walk / to go out'; the clitic particle '-то' attached to 'гулять' (гулять-то) adds contrast/emphasis ('as for going out'); 'не' negates; 'очень' is an adverb 'very'; 'пускают' is the present plural of 'пускать' meaning 'they let/allow' — together 'гулять-то не очень пускают' = 'they don't really let them go out.'
segments.p2-8-g434: 'тем более' is a fixed comparative phrase 'all the more / especially'; 'вечером' is an adverbial noun in instrumental/locative time meaning 'in the evening'; the parenthesis begins an aside: 'мне почему-то непременно казалось' — 'to me, for some reason, it necessarily seemed' ('почему-то' = 'for some reason', 'непременно' = 'certainly / absolutely', 'казалось' = past of 'казаться' 'it seemed (to me)').
segments.p2-8-g435: 'что' introduces the subordinate clause 'that she should come'; 'она' is 'she'; 'должна' is the modal/adjective 'must/ought to / is due to' indicating expectation or obligation (here 'ought to / would (be expected to)'); 'прийти' is the infinitive 'to come'; 'вечером' repeats 'in the evening'; 'и' is 'and'; 'именно' is an adverb 'exactly / precisely'; 'в' is the preposition used with time and here means 'at' (in 'at seven o'clock'); 'часов' is the genitive plural of 'час' used for hours 'o'clock'.
segments.p2-8-g326: "То-то ... то" is an emphatic exclamatory frame: the repeated particle то highlights or confirms the noun (here «свежесть почвы»). Word order is noun after the first particle for emphasis; the final то is a colloquial intensifier (roughly "just/indeed").
segments.p2-8-g327: Иногда (sometimes) sets the adverbial frame. Мне приходила мысль is an impersonal experiencer construction meaning "a thought occurred to me"; приходила is imperfective past (recurring or spontaneous occurrence). Самому modifies the agent of съездить ("to go oneself/personaly"); съездить is a perfective infinitive meaning a short visit with return, and к ней is the destination (to her).
segments.p2-8-g328: The quoted infinitive "рассказать ей все" states the content of the thought: рассказать (to tell) takes ей in the dative (indirect object) and все = "everything." И links a second infinitive (упросить) coordinated with the first; упросить is a perfective infinitive meaning "to beg/implore" and takes ее (accusative object) plus the negative infinitive не приходить (not to come) and the direction ко мне (to my place).
segments.p2-8-g329: Но тут is a short adverbial phrase meaning "but then/at that moment"; тут here functions as "at that point/moment."
segments.p2-8-g330: При этой мысли uses the preposition при + prepositional to mean "on/at the occurrence of this thought"; этой modifies мысль ("this thought").
segments.p2-8-g331: Во мне подымалась такая злоба: во (a variant of в for euphony) + мне = "within me" (location/experiencer); подымалась is an older spelling of поднималась (imperfective past) meaning "rose up/arose," and такая злоба = "such malice/anger."
segments.p2-8-g332: Что here introduces the result clause ("that ...") linking the feeling to the actions described next.
segments.p2-8-g333: Кажется is a parenthetical modal verb meaning "it seems/appears," softening the assertion that follows.
segments.p2-8-g334: Я бы + past tense verb (раздавил) forms a counterfactual conditional: "I would have crushed." Так и is an intensifying phrase here ("just/indeed so"), and эту "проклятую" Лизу is the accusative object with a contemptuous epithet.
segments.p2-8-g335: Если б (conditional если + clitic бы) introduces the protasis: она возле меня вдруг случилась — случиться (perfective past) here means "happened/appeared"; возле меня = "near me/at my side," вдруг = "suddenly."
segments.p2-8-g336: Оскорбил бы ее: another past-tense verb + бы forming the conditional series "I would have insulted her"; ее is the accusative object pronoun.
segments.p2-8-g337: Оплевал бы is another verb in the same conditional series ("would have spat on her / vilified her"); the repeated бы after each verb is stylistic and rhetorical.
segments.p2-8-g338: Выгнал бы continues the enumeration of hypothetical violent actions ("would have driven her out"); same conditional morphology.
segments.p2-8-g339: Ударил бы! — final verb in the paratactic list ("would have struck her!") with an exclamatory force; the repeated бы keeps the conditional mood.
segments.p2-8-g340: Прошел is a past perfective verb meaning "passed" and here is used elliptically with the following noun (Прошел день = "a day passed").
segments.p2-8-g341: Однако ж is a colloquial pair: однако (however/nevertheless) with the particle ж adding emphasis ("however, after all").
segments.p2-8-g342: День = "day," the noun resumed after the verb, completing the elliptical clause "a day passed."
segments.p2-8-g343: Другой = "another/other," continuing the enumeration of successive days.
segments.p2-8-g344: Третий — she did not come: третий = "a third (day)," the dash links the enumeration to the result clause она не приходила (imperfective past, indicating repeated or habitual non-arrival).
segments.p2-8-g345: И я начинал успокоиваться: and I began (начинал — imperfective past, gradual onset) to calm down (успокоиваться — reflexive, to become calm). The imperfective aspect indicates a process rather than a completed result.
segments.p2-8-g436: "А впрочем" is a discourse connective meaning "however/besides." Here "А" functions like English "but/and then" to introduce a corrective or qualifying remark, and "впрочем" reinforces that sense as "however/anyway."
segments.p2-8-g437: "она сказала" is the reporting clause in the past tense (feminine singular). "Сказала" is a past, perfective form meaning "she said," marking that what follows is direct or indirect reported speech.
segments.p2-8-g438: This subordinate clause begins with the complementizer "что" (that). "Еще не совсем" is a negated degree phrase meaning "not yet/ not completely;" "не" negates "совсем" (completely). "Там" = "there." "Закабалилась" is a reflexive past form meaning "became enslaved/subjected" (colloquial/figurative), so the whole chunk means "that she hasn't quite been enslaved there."
segments.p2-8-g439: "На особых правах состоит" is a fixed-style expression: "на + prepositional plural" (на правах) states the status/condition "on (special) rights/terms." "Особых" is the genitive/locative plural modifier "special," and "состоит" is present-tense (or general-stative) from "состоять" used here in the sense "to be registered/held in a position / to be on (special) terms" — i.e., "she is there under special privileges."
segments.p2-8-g440: "Значит" is a discourse particle here, functioning like English "so/then/that means," used to draw an inference or sum up what was just said (not the lexical "matter/be important").
segments.p2-8-g441: "Гм!" is an interjection ("hmm") expressing the speaker's ironic or doubtful reaction; it's a short vocalization with no grammatical role beyond comment.
segments.p2-8-g442: "Черт возьми" is an idiomatic exclamation equivalent to English "damn it!" Grammatically it's a frozen imperative phrase (literally 'take the devil') used as a strong interjection; treat both words together as the expletive.
segments.p2-8-g443: "Придет" is the 3rd-person singular future indicative of "прийти/прийти" (to come). Here it expresses a simple future assertion: "(she) will come."
segments.p2-8-g444: "Непременно" is an adverb meaning "certainly/without fail." The repeated "придет" (again 3rd-person singular future) is a rhetorical repetition for emphasis: "she will certainly come!"
segments.p2-8-g445: "Хорошо еще" is an evaluative phrase meaning "it's (all) the better/it's a good thing (too)." "Хорошо" functions adverbially as "good/fortunate," and "еще" here has the sense of "more/what's more" adding emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g446: The clause with "что" (that) gives what is fortunate. "Развлекал" is imperfective past (masculine singular) of "развлекать," meaning "entertained/distracted" — imperfective here stresses the ongoing/action at that moment. "Меня" is the accusative object "me." "В это время" is a temporal phrase ("at that time"), with "в" used in the temporal sense. "Аполлон" is the agent/name placed after the time phrase (word order is flexible); "своими грубостями" uses the instrumental plural to indicate means: "by his rudenesses."
segments.p2-8-g447: Из + genitive (терпенья последнего) here forms the idiom 'выводить из терпения' — to exasperate. Выводил is past imperfective, 3rd person singular, indicating a repeated or ongoing action ('was driving/exasperating'). Последнего follows терпенья for emphasis ('the last of my patience').
segments.p2-8-g399: Single-noun sentence: a bare nominative noun used as an exclamatory or summative statement. The word stands alone as the predicate/content of the sentence (Russian often omits a copula in the present).
segments.p2-8-g402: Это functions as the demonstrative/anticipatory subject ('this/that'), неделикатно is a predicative adverb meaning 'indelicate' (Russian uses no copula in present), and ну is an interjection/filler ('well') marking hesitation or mitigation.
segments.p2-8-g403: Одним словом is an idiomatic instrumental phrase meaning 'in one word' or 'in short' used to sum up what follows; словом here is literal 'word' used idiomatically.
segments.p2-8-g404: Subject я ('I') with topical adverb тут ('here/at this point'); зарапортовывался is past-tense, reflexive, imperfective verb meaning 'got carried away making a (self-)report' or 'began to make a pompous speech' and here takes в + noun phrase to mean 'into/onto (a topic)'. The cluster какой-нибудь такой is colloquial intensifying: какой-нибудь = 'some/any', такой = 'such/that kind of'; европейской is an adjective (feminine) agreeing with the later noun ('European' or 'European-style').
segments.p2-8-g405: A compound adjective formed from the name Жорж-Занд (Georges Sand) turned into an attributive adjective (hyphenated); it modifies the same feminine noun as европейской and signals a particular literary/romantic flavor ('George-Sand-like').
segments.p2-8-g406: Неизъяснимо is an adverb meaning 'inexplicably/unexplainably' modifying the adjective благородной ('noble' in feminine form). Тонкости is the noun 'subtlety/delicacy' (here in the case required by the preposition phrase with в), so the phrase means 'in an inexplicably noble subtlety.' Note agreement in gender/case with the implied/explicit noun.
segments.p2-8-g410: Но is the conjunction 'but', and теперь is the adverb 'now' (temporal), setting a contrastive temporal frame. No copula is used; the sentence begins with this contrastive marker.
segments.p2-8-g411: Repeated теперь ('now') for emphasis, dash marks a pause; ты is the pronoun subject 'you', and моя is the feminine possessive pronoun in nominative agreeing as a predicative ('you are mine'). Russian omits the verb 'to be' in the present, so the possessive stands as the predicate.
segments.p2-8-g412: Ты (subject) plus мое (neuter possessive) agrees with созданье (neuter noun, old spelling of создание) — 'you are my creation/creature'. The possessive takes the gender of the noun it modifies (созданье), not the person addressed.
segments.p2-8-g413: Чиста is a short-form feminine adjective used predicatively ('you are pure/clean'); short forms express a state and are common in literary or emphatic speech. Ты is the subject (you).
segments.p2-8-g414: Прекрасна is another short-form feminine adjective meaning 'beautiful' used predicatively; it continues the list of attributes about the addressee.
segments.p2-8-g415: Ты (subject) — dash for emphasis — followed by the full-form adjective прекрасная agreeing with жена (nominative feminine), and моя comes after the noun (жена моя), a slightly poetic/possessive word order meaning 'my wife.' Using the full-form adjective here (not the short form) plus the postposed possessive gives a warmer, emphatic tone.
segments.p2-8-g416: И links this line to what precedes; в дом мой is a slightly poetic word order (more common is в мой дом) meaning 'into my house'; смело и свободно are adverbs modifying the imperative войди ('enter!'). Хозяйкой полною is in the instrumental case expressing role/state ('as [your] full/complete mistress'), with полною showing an older/poetic adjective ending; войди is the imperative of войти ('enter').
segments.p2-8-g448: Это is a demonstrative pronoun ('this/it') introducing the clause; была is the past-tense singular feminine form of быть ('was'), agreeing in gender with the noun язва; язва моя is a noun + possessive where the possessive моя follows the noun for emphasis ('my sore').
segments.p2-8-g449: Бич is an appositive noun in the nominative that amplifies язва (joined by a comma); it names the same thing in a stronger, figurative sense ('a scourge').
segments.p2-8-g450: Посланный is a past passive participle (perfective) from послать functioning as an adjectival modifier ('sent'); на меня is the preposition на + accusative meaning 'on/upon me'; провиденьем is the instrumental case indicating the agent ('by Providence') with an archaic spelling of провидение.
segments.p2-8-g451: Мы с ним is a colloquial comitative construction meaning 'he and I' or 'we with him' as the subject; пикировались is a past-tense, plural, reflexive/imperfective verb meaning 'were sparring' or 'clashing' (not the literal 'dive' sense here); постоянно is an adverb modifying the verb ('constantly').
segments.p2-8-g452: Несколько лет is a quantitative phrase where несколько governs the genitive лет ('several years'); сряду is an adverb meaning 'in a row' or 'consecutively', modifying the time span.
segments.p2-8-g453: И is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; я is the subject 'I'; его is the accusative object 'him'; ненавидел is the past-tense masculine singular of ненавидеть ('hated').
segments.p2-8-g454: Бог мой is an exclamatory address/interjection ('my God'), with the possessive мой placed after the noun for forceful emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g455: Как here is an exclamative particle meaning 'how' (not a temporal/conditional 'as'); я is the subject 'I'; его is the object 'him'; ненавидел is the past tense 'hated', expressing strong feeling.
segments.p2-8-g456: Никого is the genitive form of никто used as the negative object ('no one/anyone' in negative constructions); в жизни is a time/experience phrase 'in (my) life'; я is the subject; ещё here has the sense 'yet/ever' anticipating the negated verb that follows.
segments.p2-8-g457: Кажется is a parenthetical present-tense verb meaning 'it seems' or 'I think', softening the statement.
segments.p2-8-g458: Так is an adverb meaning 'so' or 'to such a degree'; не is the negation particle applied to the past verb; ненавидел is the past tense 'hated' — together так не ненавидел means 'did not hate so (much)', i.e. 'have not hated anyone so (much)'.
segments.p2-8-g459: Как is the comparative conjunction 'as/like' introducing the comparison; его is the pronoun 'him' (object) being compared to others.
segments.p2-8-g460: Особенно is an adverb 'especially'; в + иные минуты is a time-expression meaning 'at certain/other moments' (в here denotes time, иные = 'other/some' modifying минуты 'moments').
segments.p2-8-g370: Наконец is an adverb meaning 'finally' or 'at last' and here sets the temporal frame. Она is the subject pronoun 'she' (nominative) placed after the adverb in normal Russian order.
segments.p2-8-g371: Вся here precedes a participle and functions as an intensifier meaning 'entirely' or 'completely' (literally 'all'); смущенная is a short participial/adjectival form 'embarrassed' describing the subject. The comma after it lists further descriptive adjectives.
segments.p2-8-g372: Прекрасная is an adjectival description 'beautiful' (feminine singular) coordinating with the other adjectival phrases; it modifies 'она' and is set off by commas for rhetorical effect.
segments.p2-8-g373: Дрожа and рыдая are present-tense gerunds/participles (деепричастия or adjectival participles used predicatively), literally 'trembling' and 'sobbing', indicating simultaneous manner of action; the conjunction и links them.
segments.p2-8-g374: Бросается is a reflexive verb form (бросаться) meaning 'throws herself' or 'rushes' (present tense). К + ногам is the dative plural 'to the feet', with моим in dative plural agreeing with ногам; the order 'ногам моим' is slightly emphatic/poetic. И говорит continues the main verb sequence 'and says'.
segments.p2-8-g375: This clause is a content clause introduced by что 'that' reporting what she says. Я is nominative 'I'; ее (here functioning as a possessive pronoun 'her') + спаситель = 'her savior/rescuer'. The second что introduces the second reported clause 'that she loves me'; меня is the accusative object 'me'. Больше всего на свете is an idiom meaning 'more than anything in the world' (больше modifies любит, всего intensifies 'of all', на свете = 'in the world').
segments.p2-8-g376: Я is the subject 'I'. Изумляюсь is first-person singular present reflexive verb 'I am astonished' (изумляться), stating the speaker's reaction; the reflexive ending marks an intransitive/state meaning.
segments.p2-8-g377: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but' or 'however'; here it stands alone at the end of the sentence for abrupt contrast or trailing thought.
segments.p2-8-g380: Лиза is the vocative use of the proper name 'Liza' (addressing her directly) and is set off by quotation marks/dashes as direct speech.
segments.p2-8-g381: Говорю я is the reporting clause 'I say' with the verb говорить in present tense; the pronoun я is added for emphasis and follows the verb (inverted order is common in spoken style).
segments.p2-8-g382: Неужели is a rhetorical particle meaning 'really?' or 'could it be?', while the enclitic particle ж adds emphasis ('after all'). Ты думаешь is the direct question 'do you think' (present tense, 2nd person singular). The whole phrase introduces incredulity.
segments.p2-8-g383: Что introduces the subordinate interrogative clause 'that I did not notice your love'. Я is subject 'I'; не + заметил is the past-tense perfective 'did not notice' (masculine speaker). Твоей любви is genitive 'your love' (possession/object of замечать in this construction).
segments.p2-8-g384: Я видел все uses the past tense видел (perfective/slightly narrative past) 'I saw' with все 'everything' as the object; together it means 'I had seen everything' or 'I saw everything'.
segments.p2-8-g385: Я угадал is past tense 'I guessed' or 'I divined' (угадать), presenting a successful inference; the short clause follows the previous one as parallel statements.
segments.p2-8-g386: Но 'but' introduces the contrast. Не смел посягать на твое сердце первый literally 'did not dare to attempt upon your heart first': смел here is intended as past tense of сметь 'to dare' (negated by не = 'did not dare'); посягать на is an infinitive 'to encroach on/lay hands on'; твое сердце is the object (your heart), and первый functions as a predicate adjective implying 'to be the first' (agreeing with the subject 'I', implied in the clause).
segments.p2-8-g387: Потому что introduces a causal clause 'because'. Имел на тебя влияние is an idiomatic expression 'had influence over you' (иметь влияние на кого), with имел as past-tense 'had' and на тебя showing the affected person. И боялся continues with 'and was afraid' (past tense боялся).
segments.p2-8-g388: Что ты begins the content of the fear: 'that you ...' with что introducing the subordinate clause and ты as the subject 'you' (singular familiar).
segments.p2-8-g389: Из благодарности is a prepositional phrase 'out of/from gratitude' explaining motive: из + noun in genitive (благодарности) = 'out of gratitude'.
segments.p2-8-g390: Нарочно 'deliberately' or 'on purpose' modifies заставишь себя 'you will force yourself' (заставишь is 2nd-person singular future of заставить 'to force/compel', себя is the reflexive 'yourself'). Отвечать на любовь мою is an infinitive phrase 'to respond/answer my love' (отвечать на + accusative), i.e. to reciprocate the speaker's love.
segments.p2-8-g391: Сама here emphasizes that she herself (by her own action) would do it; насильно 'forcibly' qualifies вызовешь (2nd-person future of вызвать 'to call forth, summon, provoke') — 'you will forcibly call forth in yourself'; в себе is a reflexive prepositional phrase 'in yourself'; чувство is the direct object 'a feeling'.
segments.p2-8-g392: Которого is the genitive singular relative pronoun referring back to чувство: 'of which' or 'that' in the sense 'a feeling which/that ...'; it links to the subordinate clause that follows.
segments.p2-8-g393: Может быть is a fixed phrase meaning 'perhaps' or 'it may be' (может = modal 'may', быть = 'be'); together they introduce the possibility that the feeling may not exist.
segments.p2-8-g394: Нет here functions as a short negative response 'no' (in the sense 'maybe not') following может быть; it's colloquial and abrupt.
segments.p2-8-g395: А introduces a slight contrast 'and/but' (here more like 'and yet'); я этого не хотел literally 'I did not want this' — этого (genitive) refers back to the described possibility, with не хотел as the past-tense negated verb 'did not want'.
segments.p2-8-g396: Потому что это is a short trailing clause 'because that (is so/that... )' where потому что 'because' introduces an unfinished justification; это is a demonstrative 'this/that' referring to the previously described outcome.
segments.p2-8-g478: Это был — an identifying construction: это acts as a demonstrative/anticipatory subject ('this/that' → here naturally 'he/it was') followed by the past-tense copula был. Педант is the predicate noun in the nominative. В высочайшей степени is an adverbial prepositional phrase (в + prepositional 'высочайшей степени') meaning 'to the highest degree' (высочайшей is a superlative adjective modifying степень).
segments.p2-8-g479: И connects this clause to the next. Самый + adjective (самый огромный) forms a clear superlative 'the most enormous.' Педант repeats the noun. Из всех is a comparative frame ('of/out of all'), where из + plural (всех) marks the set being compared.
segments.p2-8-g480: Каких begins a relative clause in the genitive plural referring back to 'всех' (literally 'of which/whom'), so: 'of all whom I had met.' Я встречал is past imperfective, indicating occurrences/habitual encounters in the past. Только here is an intensifier meaning roughly 'ever/just' in this idiom. На земле is a locative adverbial ('on earth').
segments.p2-8-g481: И при этом is a connective phrase ('and besides/at that') linking the added comment. С + instrumental (самолюбием) means 'with (a) self-love/vanity'; самолюбием is instrumental singular and supplies the manner/quality that accompanies the pedant.
segments.p2-8-g482: Приличным here is an adjective in the instrumental agreeing with the previously mentioned самолюбием ('with a vanity that was becoming/proper'). Разве только is an idiomatic particle meaning 'perhaps only' or 'except only.' Александру Македонскому is in the dative: the phrase means 'befitting perhaps only Alexander of Macedon' (i.e. Alexander the Great).
segments.p2-8-g483: Был влюблен is a stative past construction (быть + short-form adjective/participle) meaning 'was enamored.' В takes the accusative here (влюблен в X = 'in love with X'). Каждую пуговицу свою: каждую (accusative feminine singular) + свою (reflexive possessive agreeing with subject 'he') emphasizes each individual button.
segments.p2-8-g484: This repeats the same construction (влюблен) after a prepositional phrase 'в каждый свой ноготь' where каждый + noun is accusative masculine singular; свой again refers back to the subject. The dash and the adverb непременно ('without fail/certainly') add emphasis before repeating the predicate.
segments.p2-8-g485: Тем is an adverbial demonstrative meaning 'by that' / 'thus' linking the previous clauses to the verb смотрел. Смотрел is past imperfective 'looked' or 'he looked/displayed himself that way.' The word order (он тем смотрел) is emphatic: 'that's how he looked.'
segments.p2-8-g486: Относился (относиться) is past imperfective reflexive 'treated/related' in 3rd person singular; the verb-first order 'Относился он' is slightly emphatic. Ко + dative (ко мне) is the prepositional variant used before 'мне' meaning 'toward me.' Вполне деспотически is an adverbial phrase ('completely/despotically') modifying the verb.
segments.p2-8-g487: Чрезвычайно мало is an adverbial quantifier ('extremely little/very seldom'). Говорил is past imperfective 'spoke/talked' and here indicates he scarcely conversed; со мной is the prepositional/instrumental phrase 'with me' (со is used before мной).
segments.p2-8-g488: А если случалось ему ... is an impersonal construction: случалось (it happened) + dative ему = 'if it happened to him.' На меня взглядывать uses на + accusative to mean 'to glance at me'; взглядывать is iterative 'to glance (now and then)' rather than a single perfective glance.
segments.p2-8-g489: То is the correlative particle in the conditional 'если ... то' ('if ... then'), so 'то смотрел' = 'then he looked.' Твердым is an instrumental adjective used adverbially with смотреть to describe manner: 'looked with a firm (expression).' The instrumental case marks the manner or means.
segments.p2-8-g490: This whole phrase is an instrumental-phrase of manner describing the look: величаво (an adverb 'majestically') modifies самоуверенным (instrumental adjective 'self-confident'), and постоянно насмешливым ('constantly mocking') further qualifies взглядом ('with a gaze' — взглядом is instrumental singular). Together: 'with a majestically self-assured and constantly mocking gaze.'
segments.p2-8-g491: Приводившим is a past participle in instrumental singular agreeing with взглядом (the participle modifies 'gaze'): 'a gaze that was causing/which would bring.' Меня is the object, иногда 'sometimes' is adverbial, and в бешенство is the result phrase 'into fury/rage' (в + accusative). Together: 'which sometimes drove me into a rage.'
segments.p2-8-g492: Исполнял is the imperfective past tense (he was performing/used to perform), placed before the pronoun он for slight emphasis or stylistic inversion. Свою должность is the direct object (своя agrees with он), and с таким видом uses the preposition с + instrumental (видом) to mean “with such an air/appearance.” The imperfective verb here stresses habitual or characteristic manner rather than a single completed act.
segments.p2-8-g493: Как будто is a fixed conjunctional phrase meaning “as if.” Делал is the imperfective past of делать (here ‘was doing’), taking мне in the dative as the beneficiary and высочайшую милость in the accusative as the thing done; высочайшую is a superlative adjective. The whole clause is ironical: he behaved as though he were conferring a supreme favour on me.
segments.p2-8-g461: The noun 'Человек' is fronted as a topicalizing nominative ('a man'), followed by the emphatic pronoun 'он' (he) and the past tense copula 'был' + predicate adjective 'пожилой'. The unusual order (noun + pronoun + copula) places emphasis on the person as a type: 'He was a man — elderly.'
segments.p2-8-g462: Single adjective standing after the first clause, describing his manner: 'важный' here means self-important or pompous rather than merely 'important'; it further characterizes the same noun.
segments.p2-8-g463: 'Занимавшийся' is a past active participle (from 'заниматься') functioning as an adjectival phrase describing the man: 'engaged in' or 'practising'. It takes the instrumental noun 'портняжеством' (tailoring) and is modified by the adverb 'отчасти' (partly). The reflexive aspect of 'заниматься' simply marks the activity.
segments.p2-8-g464: 'Но' is the conjunction 'but'. The phrase 'неизвестно почему' is an impersonal expression meaning 'it is unknown why' or 'for reasons unknown' and functions adverbially to qualify the following clause; the comma marks a slight pause.
segments.p2-8-g465: 'Он' is the explicit subject 'he'; 'презирал' is the past imperfective of 'презирать' meaning 'despised/scorned' (imperfective here indicates habitual or sustained feeling); 'меня' is the accusative object 'me'.
segments.p2-8-g466: 'Даже' is an intensifier 'even'; 'сверх' is a preposition meaning 'beyond' and governs 'всякой меры' (genitive). 'Всякой меры' literally 'any measure' or 'all moderation' — the idiom means 'to an excessive degree'.
segments.p2-8-g467: Coordinated clause: 'и' = 'and'; 'смотрел' is past imperfective 'looked' or 'regarded'; 'на меня' = 'at me'; 'нестерпимо' is an adverb 'unbearably' modifying the manner; 'свысока' is an adverbial fixed phrase 'from on high / condescendingly' (смотреть свысока = to look down on). Together: 'and looked down on me unbearably.'
segments.p2-8-g468: 'Впрочем' is a discourse adverb meaning 'however / besides / moreover' used to qualify or add to the previous remark; here it introduces a generalizing comment.
segments.p2-8-g469: Simple clause: 'он' subject, 'на всех' ('at/on everyone') with preposition + accusative indicating the object of his gaze/attitude, and 'смотрел свысока' is the fixed verb phrase 'looked down on [people]'.
segments.p2-8-g470: 'Взглянуть' is an infinitive used as an exclamatory 'just look' or 'take a look'; 'только' intensifies it as 'just/only'; 'на эту белобрысую' is the accusative feminine demonstrative phrase 'at this fair‑haired [one]' (elliptical: the noun 'head' follows). The order gives an imperative/attentional tone.
segments.p2-8-g471: 'Гладко' is an adverb 'smoothly / neatly' modifying the past passive participle 'причесанную' (combed), which together modify 'голову' in the accusative: 'a neatly combed head.' The participial adjective agrees in gender/number/case with 'голову'.
segments.p2-8-g472: Prepositional phrase 'на этот кок' ('at this chignon'): 'на' + accusative indicates direction/target of the look; 'этот кок' identifies the hair‑style feature.
segments.p2-8-g473: Relative clause introduced by 'который' ('which/that') modifying 'кок'. 'Он' is the subject; 'взбивал' (imperfective past) means 'teased up/ruffled' or 'fluffed' (hair); 'себе' is a dative/reflexive indicating he did it for himself; 'на лбу' = 'on his forehead'; 'и подмасливал' = 'and greased/oiled'; 'постным маслом' uses 'постным' (Lenten/plain) + 'маслом' (instrumental) = 'with plain/cheap oil'. The imperfective verbs give a habitual or habitual-aspect image of his grooming.
segments.p2-8-g474: Another prepositional accusative phrase 'на этот солидный рот' — 'at this solid/respectable mouth' — where 'солидный' characterizes the mouth as substantial or dignified; again the 'на' marks the target of attention.
segments.p2-8-g475: 'Всегда' = 'always'; 'сложенный' is a past participle from 'сложить' used adjectivally here to mean 'set' or 'formed'; 'ижицей' is an instrumental noun referring to the old Cyrillic letter 'ижица', used metaphorically to describe the shape of his mouth. The phrase means 'a mouth always set like an izhitsa' (an archaic image).
segments.p2-8-g476: Dash + conjunction 'и' links to the concluding result: 'and you (вы) already (уже) felt (чувствовали) before you (перед собой) a being (существо)'. 'Перед собой' is a reflexive construction meaning 'in front of you' or 'in your presence'. The past plural 'чувствовали' agrees with polite/plural 'вы'.
segments.p2-8-g477: 'Не сомневавшееся' is a negative past active participle ('not doubting') modifying 'существо'; 'в себе' = 'in itself' (i.e., in itself/itself = confident in itself); 'никогда' = 'never'. Together they form the attributive clause 'a being that never doubted itself.'
segments.p2-8-g494: Впрочем is a sentence‑initial discourse particle meaning 'however' / 'besides' that signals a contrast or qualification to the previous statement; it does not affect the sentence grammar beyond linking ideas.
segments.p2-8-g495: The clause is a sequence of coordinated parts. Он is the subject (3rd person). Почти ровно is a two-word adverbial intensifier (почти = 'almost', ровно = 'exactly/precisely' used here as an emphatic modifier) giving the sense 'practically/virtually'. Ничего is the negative pronoun 'nothing' (used as the object), followed by для меня 'for me' (benefactive). Не делал is the past imperfective negative 'did not do' (habitual/persistent non-action). И даже вовсе не считал connects with 'and even did not at all consider' — считал себя обязанным is a fixed construction meaning 'considered himself obliged' (себя + adjective in accusative expressing the result of считать). Что-нибудь делать is the infinitive complement with the indefinite что-нибудь ('anything') after a negative, giving 'to do anything.'
segments.p2-8-g496: Сомнения быть не могло is an impersonal past construction meaning 'there could be no doubt.' Сомнения is nominative (literally 'doubts'), быть (infinitive) + не могло (neuter past of мочь) yields the impersonal modal 'was not possible' → 'could not be.'
segments.p2-8-g497: That-clause introduced by что reports his opinion: он (subject) считал (past 'considered') меня (accusative 'me') за functions with считать as 'to regard/consider X as Y' (за = 'as/for'). Самого последнего is an intensive superlative 'the very last/worst,' modifying дурака (accusative 'fool'). На всем свете is a prepositional phrase 'in the whole world.'
segments.p2-8-g498: И если introduces a conditional 'and if.' Держал (past of держать) + меня (accusative) при себе is an idiom meaning 'kept me with him / kept me on (his payroll)' — держал is 'kept/retained,' and при себе ('by himself') indicates proximity or being retained in his service.
segments.p2-8-g499: То is a resumptive/linking particle ('then/so'), here pointing back to the conditional; единственно, потому, только form an emphatic chain meaning 'solely/for that reason only.' The sequence is slightly pleonastic for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g500: The subordinate clause gives the reason: что introduces 'that'; от меня 'from me' (meaning 'because of me / via me'); можно было получать is an impersonal modal past construction 'one could receive / it was possible to receive' (можно + было + infinitive получать), and каждый месяц жалованье is the object 'a salary every month' — жалованье is an older word for salary.
segments.p2-8-g501: Соглашался (past imperfective) 'agreed/consented' is followed by the quoted infinitive phrase 'ничего не делать' ('to do nothing') as the thing he agreed to. У меня here means 'in my employ/at my house' (possession/association), and за семь рублей в месяц is the payment expression 'for seven rubles a month' (за + amount = in exchange for).
segments.p2-8-g502: This is an older/colloquial impersonal construction: Мне (dative 'to me') + за него ('because of him / on his account') indicates the beneficiary and the reason; много (genitive quantifier) modifies грехов (genitive 'of sins'). Простится here is archaic/idiomatic passive meaning 'will be forgiven to' (i.e., 'will be forgiven me'), not the more common 'to say goodbye' sense; the construction is equivalent to 'мне будет прощено много грехов.'
segments.p2-8-g503: Доходило is an impersonal past-tense form (neuter) of доходить meaning 'it reached / came to (a point)'; acompañado by иногда ('sometimes'). До + genitive (такой ненависти) expresses 'to such [a state] of hatred' — ненависти is genitive singular as the object of до.
segments.p2-8-g504: Что introduces a result clause ('that...'). Меня is the accusative experiencer 'me'; бросало is imperfective past used impersonally to indicate a recurrent/physical reaction ('would throw/was throwing me'); чуть не is an idiom meaning 'almost'; в судороги uses в + accusative with the motion/result sense 'into convulsions'; от одной его походки gives the cause with от + genitive ('from/just from a single one of his strides/gait'), where одной is an indefinite/singular modifier of походки.
segments.p2-8-g505: Но 'but' contrasts the thought; особенно 'especially' intensifies гадко, which is a predicative adverb meaning 'disgusting/repulsive.' Было мне is the common Russian construction with a dative experiencer (мне) — 'it was to me' — and его пришепетывание ('his whispering') is the nominal subject/thing complained of.
segments.p2-8-g506: У него был is the possessive construction 'he had' (у + genitive indicates possession). Язык is the noun 'tongue' (literal organ here), modified by the comparative длиннее 'longer' with несколько weakening it to 'somewhat/rather long(er).' The comparative is left without an explicit standard until the next clause.
segments.p2-8-g507: Чем introduces the comparative clause ('than...'); следует here is impersonal/prescriptive 'is proper / ought to be,' so the phrase means 'longer than is proper.'
segments.p2-8-g508: Или что-то вроде этого is a parenthetical 'or something like that' — что-то (something) + вроде (like/similar to) + этого (this, genitive). The whole phrase softens or qualifies the preceding exactness.
segments.p2-8-g509: Оттого 'therefore' gives the consequence; он is the subject 'he.' Постоянно is an adverb of habitual action 'constantly.' Шепелявил and сюсюкал are past imperfective verbs describing habitual manner ('lisped' and 'cooed' respectively). The final и links on to the following clause.
segments.p2-8-g510: Кажется is a parenthetical verb 'it seems / apparently,' marking the narrator's uncertainty or reported impression.
segments.p2-8-g511: Этим is the demonstrative pronoun in the instrumental case ('by/with this' or 'of this') governed by гордился, because гордиться takes the instrumental: 'to be proud of.' Ужасно here intensifies the verb ('terribly/extremely'), and гордился is past imperfective 'was proud of.'
segments.p2-8-g512: Воображая is a деепричастие (adverbial participle) 'imagining,' indicating an attendant action or the content of his pride — it links to the following subordinate clause.
segments.p2-8-g513: Что introduces the content clause ('that this imparts ...'). Это is the subject 'this'; придает is present tense 'imparts / gives'; ему is the dative 'to him'; чрезвычайно modifies много достоинства ('an extraordinarily great amount of dignity'), with достоинства in the genitive governed by много.
segments.p2-8-g514: Говорил is past-tense 'spoke' and the verb-first order here (Говорил он) gives a narrative tone; тихо is the adverb describing manner 'quietly.'
segments.p2-8-g515: Размеренно is an adverb ('measuredly / with measured cadence') further describing the manner of speaking; it stands as a second adverbial modifier after тихо.
segments.p2-8-g516: Заложив and опустив are adverbial participles (деепричастия) expressing attendant actions: 'having placed / placing his hands behind his back' and 'having lowered his eyes.' Руки is accusative plural (object of заложив), за + accusative (спину) marks position 'behind the back'; опустив takes глаза (accusative) and в + accusative (в землю) marks direction/toward 'to the ground.'
segments.p2-8-g531: Но тогда — 'but then' (contrast + temporal), setting the time/situation. Я не мог — past tense of мочь with negation (imperfective past + не) expresses inability in that past situation ('I could not'). Прогнать его — infinitive (perfective) following мог to indicate the specific action he could not accomplish ('drive him away'); standard word order with the object after the verb. The comma links this clause to the comparative/illustrative clause that follows.
segments.p2-8-g532: Точно here is a comparative particle meaning 'as if'/'as though' rather than 'exactly'. Он был слит — passive past (быть + short participle слит) meaning 'he was merged/fused'; слит is a perfective participle from слить and expresses a completed or resultant state. С существованием моим uses с + instrumental (существованием) to mean 'with my existence'; the possessive моим follows the noun (postposed) in a literary order for emphasis. Химически is an adverb modifying слит and placed at the end for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g533: К тому же is a fixed phrase meaning 'besides/moreover'. Он бы ... согласился uses the particle бы with the past tense согласился to form a conditional ('would have agreed'); the negation не before the verb makes it 'would not have agreed.' И сам is emphatic ('even himself' / 'he himself') — и functions as an intensifier 'even' here and сам emphasizes the person. От меня уйти is the infinitive phrase 'to leave me' with от + genitive indicating leaving away from someone. Ни за что is an idiom meaning 'for nothing/under no circumstances' (ни intensifies the negation + за что = 'for anything').
segments.p2-8-g534: Мне нельзя было жить — impersonal construction: нельзя + past было with dative мне expresses that it was impossible or not permitted for me ('I couldn't'). Жить is the infinitive 'to live/reside.' В шамбр-гарни is a loan phrase (chambre garnie) meaning a small furnished room; the foreign compound is used as the locative of residence.
segments.p2-8-g535: Моя квартира была мой особняк — a nominal equative clause: subject 'моя квартира' (feminine) + past copula была + predicate noun 'мой особняк' (masculine), so the possessive agrees with the predicate noun (мой) rather than the subject; meaning 'my apartment was my mansion.' Word order emphasizes the subject then the identifying predicate.
segments.p2-8-g536: Моя скорлупа — short appositional noun phrase 'my shell,' part of a list of metaphors for the apartment; straightforward possessive + noun.
segments.p2-8-g537: Мой футляр — another appositive item ('my case'/'my sheath'), masculine possessive my + noun; continues the metaphorical series describing the apartment.
segments.p2-8-g538: В который я прятался — relative clause: 'в который' (into which) uses в + accusative because the verb прятался (motion toward/into the interior) is implied; я прятался is past reflexive 'I hid myself.' От всего человечества uses от + genitive to mean 'from all humanity.' The clause modifies футляр ('the case into which I hid myself from all humanity').
segments.p2-8-g539: А introduces a contrast/shift ('and'/'but'), and 'Аполлон' is the nominative subject (a person nicknamed or likened to Apollo). The comma signals a parenthetical explanation to follow.
segments.p2-8-g540: Черт знает почему is an idiomatic parenthetical 'devil/God knows why' meaning 'for reasons I can't explain.' Черт is an expletive noun, знает is present 'knows,' and почему is 'why.' The phrase expresses uncertainty or inexplicability.
segments.p2-8-g541: Казался мне принадлежащим к этой квартире — казался мне ('seemed to me') uses dative мне to mark the experiencer. Принадлежащим is a participial/gerundial form meaning 'belonging' used as a nominal complement; the construction принадлежащим к + dative (к этой квартире) means 'belonging to that apartment' (k + dative here expresses 'to/with respect to' the apartment). The whole clause means 'he seemed to me to belong to that apartment.'
segments.p2-8-g542: И я целых семь лет не мог согнать его — 'and I for a full seven years could not drive him off.' Целых is an emphatic quantifier meaning 'as many as'/'a whole' (stress on duration). Семь лет is a time expression (years, genitive). Не мог + perfective согнать expresses past inability to perform that specific single action ('couldn't manage to drive him away'). Его is the direct object 'him.'
segments.p2-8-g543: Задержать is the perfective infinitive ‘to delay/hold back’ used as the verbal noun/subject of the impersonal construction that follows. Its perfective aspect emphasizes the completed action (the act of delaying pay).
segments.p2-8-g544: Например is a parenthetical adverb ‘for example,’ marking the forthcoming instance; it does not affect the sentence’s core grammar.
segments.p2-8-g545: Его жалованье is the object of задержать: his salary/pay (его = his). Хотя/хоть here begins a correlative numerical phrase; in context хоть два, хоть три (ellipsis of the repeated noun) expresses ‘even two, even three’ days — a concessive/quantity sense. The numeral and noun are tied to the following segment.
segments.p2-8-g546: Хоть три дня continues the correlative (‘even for three days’); дня is the form required with numerals like три. Было невозможно is an impersonal past construction (= ‘it was impossible’) whose logical subject is the infinitive Задержать earlier, so the whole means delaying the pay for even two or three days could not be done.
segments.p2-8-g672: This is a rhetorical question built around the modal verb смеешь (2nd person singular present of сметь) meaning 'dare'. Как here introduces the challenge—'how (dare)'. The verb смеешь is followed by the infinitive входить ('to enter'), and the phrase ко мне ('toward/to me', i.e. 'into my room') uses the dative with the preposition ко. Без спросу is an idiomatic genitive phrase 'without asking (permission)'. The coordinate clause и так глядеть на меня uses the infinitive глядеть with на + accusative to mark the object ('look at me') and так modifies глядеть as 'in that way/like that.'
segments.p2-8-g673: Отвечай is the singular imperative of отвечать ('to answer'), a direct command. Tone is forceful; context makes it a demand for a reply rather than the intransitive 'correspond'.
segments.p2-8-g674: Посмотрев is a perfective adverbial participle (literally 'having looked' / 'after looking') that links this subordinate adverbial clause to the main clause; it indicates a completed action that precedes the main verb. На меня is the object of смотреть (expressed with на + accusative = 'at me'). Спокойно is an adverb modifying the manner of looking. С полминуты is a colloquial temporal phrase ('for/about half a minute') introduced by с.
segments.p2-8-g675: Он is the subject 'he'. Снова is an adverb 'again'. Начал is the past tense (masculine singular) of начать 'began' and is followed by the imperfective reflexive infinitive повертываться ('to turn around'), so the construction means 'he began to begin/do the action (and it was ongoing or habitual).' The reflexive ending -ся on повертываться marks the intransitive/middle voice 'turn oneself/turn around.'
segments.p2-8-g676: Стой is the imperative singular of стоять (or an irregular imperative form used as a command) meaning 'stop' or 'halt' in spoken commands. It is an abrupt order to cease movement or remain in place.
segments.p2-8-g625: Часа через два is a colloquial temporal phrase meaning “in about two hours” (часа is the genitive singular used after через to indicate approximate time). Вдруг is an adverb “suddenly” modifying the verbs that follow. Опять = “again.” Выйдет and появится are perfective, third-person singular, simple-future forms (выйдет = ‘will go out/come out’, появится = ‘will appear’); the pair expresses two successive, single actions. Так же (так + же) means “in the same way” or “just the same,” and передо мной places the action spatially “in front of me / before me.” Word order puts the time phrase first for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g626: Случалось is the impersonal/habitual past form of случаться meaning “it happened” or “it would happen”; here it sets a recurring circumstance (English: “it would happen / sometimes”).
segments.p2-8-g627: Что here is the subordinating conjunction “that” introducing the subordinate clause; я is the first-person subject (“I”). The comma after я marks a short pause before the descriptive phrase that follows.
segments.p2-8-g628: В бешенстве is a state phrase: в + noun in the prepositional (or old locative-looking) form meaning “in a fury / in a rage.” It describes the speaker’s emotional condition.
segments.p2-8-g629: Уж и не спрашивал is a colloquial past construction: уж (an intensifier, here “already”/“even”) + и (also/emphatic) + не (negation) + спрашивал (imperfective past “asked”), meaning “wouldn’t even ask.” Его is the object “him.” The whole phrase shows the speaker’s refusal/evasion of the question in anger.
segments.p2-8-g630: Чего is the colloquial/genitive question form of что meaning “what” (used with надо). Ему is the dative “to him.” Надо is an impersonal predicate meaning “is needed / is necessary” and with a dative gives “what does he need?” — idiomatically “what does he want?”
segments.p2-8-g631: А introduces a contrast (“but/and”). Просто сам combines просто (“simply/merely”) with сам used emphatically (“I myself” / “myself”); сам emphasizes the subject. Резко и повелительно are adverbs modifying подымал, meaning “sharply and imperiously.” Подымал is an imperfective past form (habitual/repeated action) of подымать (an older variant of поднимать) meaning “lift/raise” (here, the head). Голову is the direct object in accusative. И тоже начинал смотреть на него в упор continues the action: начинал (past imperfective “would begin to”), смотреть на него (“to look at him”), and в упор is an idiom meaning “fixedly/intently.”
segments.p2-8-g632: Так is a manner adverb “thus / that way.” Смотрим is present tense form of смотреть but in this narrative it functions as a habitual/presentive (with бывало) describing recurring behavior (“we look / we would look”). Мы is the explicit subject; placing the verb before the pronoun is a stylistic, colloquial/informal ordering.
segments.p2-8-g633: Бывало is an adverb meaning “it used to happen / sometimes / would (habitually).” It marks that the scene just described is a repeated or typical occurrence.
segments.p2-8-g634: Друг на друга is the reciprocal phrase “at one another / at each other” (друг = ‘other’ here). Минуты две is a duration phrase where the noun in the genitive (минуты) plus a numeral after it gives the duration “two minutes” (word order is flexible and here places the duration after the reciprocal phrase).
segments.p2-8-g635: Наконец is “finally / at last.” Он is the subject “he.” Повернется is the perfective, third-person singular future (or narrative present with future force) of повернуться, here “turns (round) / will turn,” describing the next action in the habitual sequence.
segments.p2-8-g636: Медленно и важно are adverbs modifying how he turns: медленно = “slowly,” важно = “with importance / gravely / with dignity” (describing demeanor). The pair characterizes the deliberate, solemn manner of his movement.
segments.p2-8-g637: И уйдет опять на два часа: и = “and”; уйдет is perfective future “will leave / goes away”; опять = “again”; на два часа is a phrase of duration introduced by на meaning “for (a period of) two hours.” Together: “and (then) he will go away again for two hours.”
segments.p2-8-g547: Он (he) is the subject; бы is the conditional particle that marks a hypothetical action; такую завел историю is a colloquial phrase where такую (such) modifies историю (story/scene) and завел (perfective past of завести) means 'started/raised' (here: 'caused a fuss' or 'made a scene'). The clause is an independent conditional main clause that sets up the result clause beginning with что.
segments.p2-8-g548: что introduces the result clause ('that'); я (I) is subject; бы again is the conditional particle repeated in the subordinate clause; не знал is the past tense of знать with a negation, giving 'would not have known' or 'wouldn't know'.
segments.p2-8-g549: куда (where/to where) begins a short elliptical infinitival phrase completing 'не знал' (I wouldn't know where); и here functions as a connective/emphatic 'or even' inside the idiom куда и деваться — a fixed expression meaning 'where to go/what to do'; деваться is the infinitive meaning 'to go away / to get out' used reflexively in idiom sense 'to have no escape/way out'.
segments.p2-8-g550: Но (but) begins the sentence; в эти дни (in these days) gives the time frame; я был на всех озлоблен uses был + short-form participle озлоблен to mean 'I was embittered/angry at everyone'. до того here is an intensifying part of the phrase 'до того ... что' = 'so... that' (i.e. 'so embittered that...').
segments.p2-8-g551: что introduces the consequence clause; решился is the perfective past of решиться and here means 'decided (to do something)'; the comma indicates the clause continues with the infinitive phrase that follows.
segments.p2-8-g552: почему-то (for some reason) and для чего-то (for some purpose) are adverbial expressions formed with the particle -то giving vagueness/indefiniteness; и simply links the two vague phrases. These do not state a clear motive — they mean 'for some reason, for some purpose.'
segments.p2-8-g553: наказать (infinitive) is the complement of решился — 'decided to punish'; Аполлона is the accusative object (the clerk Apollon); и links the coordinated infinitive clause не выдавать ему еще две недели жалованья — 'and not to hand him his pay for another two weeks.' еще две недели modifies жалованья to mean 'an additional/two more weeks.'
segments.p2-8-g554: Я давно уж — я is subject; давно уж is a time phrase 'for a long time / for some time now' (уж adds emphasis: 'already'). This frames the speaker's long-standing intention.
segments.p2-8-g555: года два is a colloquial time expression meaning 'about two years' (года is the numeral form used in colloquial counting here + два).
segments.p2-8-g556: собирался (imperfective past) means 'was intending/kept meaning to'; это сделать is the infinitive object 'to do this'; единственно (only) qualifies the purpose introduced by чтоб ('in order that') and доказать ему is the purpose 'to prove to him.'
segments.p2-8-g557: что introduces a content clause: 'that he не смеет так уж важничать надо мной' — he does not (не) dare/has no right to put on airs (важничать) toward me (надо мной). The second что begins another clause introduced by если (if) with захочу (future in the sense 'should I want / if I want') continuing the conditional thought.
segments.p2-8-g558: то (then/particle in conditional) introduces the result of the conditional: всегда могу (I can always) + infinitive не выдать ему жалованья — 'refuse to give him his pay.' The construction всегда могу + infinitive states a general ability or possibility.
segments.p2-8-g559: Я положил here is colloquial past 'I resolved/decided' (положил себя сделать = 'laid it down for myself'); не говорить ему об этом and даже нарочно молчать are coordinated infinitives giving the intended behavior ('not to tell him about it and even to be deliberately silent').
segments.p2-8-g560: чтоб (in order that) introduces purpose: победить его гордость — 'to overcome/defeat his pride' (победить can mean overcome a feeling); и заставить его самого is the second purpose 'and to force him himself' (его самого emphasizes that it must be he personally).
segments.p2-8-g561: первого is an appositive/parenthetical ordinal 'the first' (he should be the first to speak) — a stylistic emphasis placed after 'самого' to mark him as the very first to speak.
segments.p2-8-g562: заговорить (perfective, infinitive) means 'to begin to speak' — here it is the expected reaction: to 'begin to speak about' — followed by the preposition о + жалованье 'about the salary.'
segments.p2-8-g657: "закричал я в исступлении" is a past-tense perfective verb with the subject placed after it for emphasis: "I cried out." The phrase "в исступлении" (prepositional case after "в") is an adverbial phrase meaning "in a frenzy" describing the manner of the action.
segments.p2-8-g658: "когда" introduces a temporal subordinate clause ("when/while"). "повертывался" is the imperfective past tense (reflexive form of "повернуть/повертываться") indicating an ongoing or repeated action: "was turning/turned." The adverbs "медленно и молча" precede the verb as manner modifiers: "slowly and silently."
segments.p2-8-g659: "с одной рукой за спиной" is a compound prepositional phrase describing posture: "with one hand behind (his) back." "с" governs the instrumental "рукой" (accompaniment/position), and "за спиной" uses "за" + noun in instrumental to mean "behind the back." "одной" agrees with "рукой."
segments.p2-8-g660: "чтоб уйти" uses the subordinate-purpose particle "чтоб" + perfective infinitive "уйти" to express intent: "in order to leave." "в свою комнату" is motion into a place; "свою" is the reflexive possessive referring back to the clause's subject (he): "into his own room."
segments.p2-8-g661: "стой" is a direct imperative, a one-word command meaning "stop/halt!" addressed to the person being spoken to; here it functions as an exclamation at the end of the sentence.
segments.p2-8-g662: "воротись" is the colloquial imperative (2nd person singular) of the reflexive verb "воротиться," meaning "come back/turn back." The reflexive ending -сь indicates motion back toward the previous place or state.
segments.p2-8-g663: A repetition of the same imperative "воротись," used for emphasis; grammatically identical to the previous segment and functioning as an urgent command.
segments.p2-8-g664: "говорю я тебе" places the verb "говорю" (present 1st person) before the subject "я" to add emphasis: "I tell (you)." "тебе" is the dative of address: "to you" (recipient of the speech).
segments.p2-8-g665: "и" is the coordinating conjunction "and," here used to connect this remark to the previous thought; placed at the start it has a colloquial linking function.
segments.p2-8-g666: "должно быть" is a parenthetical modal expression meaning "it must be/probably." Grammatically it consists of "должно" (modal/impersonal) + infinitive "быть" but functions as an adverbial comment expressing supposition.
segments.p2-8-g667: "я так неестественно рявкнул" is a past-tense clause: "I barked/snapped so unnaturally." "рявкнул" is a perfective past verb (onomatopoetic) meaning a sudden loud exclamation; "так неестественно" is an adverbial intensifier placed before the verb.
segments.p2-8-g668: "что" introduces the result clause "that he turned..."; "он повернулся" is a simple past action "he turned around." The construction "стал + infinitive" ("стал меня разглядывать") means "began to [look at me]," with "разглядывать" imperfective to indicate the continuing action; "с некоторым удивлением" is an adverbial phrase "with some surprise."
segments.p2-8-g669: "Впрочем" is a sentence-initial parenthetical adverb meaning "however/anyway," used to qualify or soften what follows; it does not alter the core clause's grammar.
segments.p2-8-g670: "продолжал не говорить ни слова" uses the past imperfective "продолжал" + infinitive "не говорить" to mean "kept not speaking." The idiom "ни слова" (with the negative particle "ни") means "not a word/no single utterance."
segments.p2-8-g671: "а это-то меня и бесило" contrasts with "а" (but), uses emphatic "это-то" (demonstrative + particle "-то" = "this very/that exactly"), and an impersonal past "бесило" where the thing (это) is the grammatical subject and "меня" is the experiencer: "that was what infuriated me." The particle "и" here adds colloquial emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g600: Conditional conjunction introducing a subordinate 'if' clause; it sets up a hypothetical or habitual condition for the rest of the sentence.
segments.p2-8-g601: Parenthetical adverb meaning 'for example' or 'say', set off by commas and giving an illustrative nuance to the conditional clause.
segments.p2-8-g602: Two coordinated past-imperfective verbs with the first-person subject 'я'. 'Выдерживал' (imperfective past) indicates enduring or putting up with (habitual or repeated past action); 'делал' + noun 'вид' forms the idiom 'делал вид' = 'pretended' (literally 'made an appearance'). Together they describe what the subject used to do in such situations.
segments.p2-8-g603: 'Что' is a complementizer introducing the content clause (what was being pretended). The clause uses the present-tense verb 'не замечаю' as the reported content of the pretence ('pretended that I do not notice'), a common Russian pattern where the inner clause describes the supposed or quoted state. 'Этих взглядов' is the genitive plural of 'взгляд' (those looks).
segments.p2-8-g604: Personal pronoun 'он' introduces the main clause subject (he) and contrasts with the subordinate 'I' subject; placed before the adverbial phrase for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g605: Adverbial phrase 'по-прежнему молча' modifies the main verb: 'по-прежнему' (literally 'according to the former') means 'as before' or 'still', and 'молча' means 'silently'. Both are adverbial modifiers of manner/continuity placed before the verb they qualify.
segments.p2-8-g606: Imperfective past 'приступал' (from 'приступать') describes the habitual or repeated action 'would begin/proceed to' in past narrative; 'приступать к' governs a dative object, hence 'к дальнейшим истязаниям' ('to further torments') uses the dative plural. The phrase describes the next stage of his behavior.
segments.p2-8-g607: Adverb 'вдруг' meaning 'suddenly' that signals an abrupt action and introduces the episode; often used with narrative-perfective verbs to mark sudden entrance.
segments.p2-8-g608: Parenthetical 'бывало' indicates habitual occurrence in the narrative ('it would happen / as was customary'); it softens the suddenness by marking the action as something that used to occur repeatedly.
segments.p2-8-g609: Fixed idiomatic negational phrase 'ни с того ни с сего' ('from neither that nor this') meaning 'out of the blue' or 'for no reason at all'. The repeated particles 'ни ... ни ...' plus genitives form the idiom.
segments.p2-8-g610: Perfective verb form 'войдет' (from 'войти') here is used in a habitual narrative pattern (with 'бывало') to mean 'he would enter' on repeated occasions; 'тихо и плавно' are manner adverbs 'quietly and smoothly'; 'в мою комнату' is a motion-to phrase with preposition 'в' + accusative ('into my room').
segments.p2-8-g611: Temporal subordinate clause introduced by 'когда' ('when'); present-tense verbs 'я хожу или читаю' describe the ongoing activity at the time of his entrance ('when I am walking (pacing) or reading'). The verbs are imperfective present, marking habitual or simultaneous action.
segments.p2-8-g612: Perfective/future-form verb 'остановится' (3rd sing.) describes the immediate subsequent action 'he stops'; 'у дверей' uses preposition 'у' with the genitive plural 'дверей' to mean 'at/by the door'.
