segments.p2-8-g613: 'Заложит' is perfective 3rd-person singular from 'заложить' and here describes a punctual gesture 'will put/lay' — idiomatically 'put one hand behind his back'; 'одну руку' is the accusative object, and 'за спину' is the directional phrase 'behind his back'.
segments.p2-8-g614: Two coordinated perfective verbs: 'отставит' ('will set back / put back') describes moving the foot back, and 'устремит' ('will direct/aim') governs 'на меня' to indicate he directs his gaze at me. 'Свой взгляд' is a possessive construction ('his own gaze') and 'на меня' is the object of that directed look.
segments.p2-8-g615: Colloquial concessive/intensifying phrase 'уж не то что' introduces a contrast: 'not so much X' (here 'строгий' = 'strict') — 'уж' intensifies the negation; the construction prepares the stronger negative statement that follows.
segments.p2-8-g616: Contrastive conjunction 'а' ('but') introduces the stronger characterization; 'совсем' is an adverb 'completely/utterly' intensifying the adjective 'презрительный' ('contemptuous'), which functions predicatively (describe the quality of the gaze).
segments.p2-8-g617: Conditional clause opener 'Если' + subject 'я' and adverb 'вдруг' ('suddenly'); 'спрошу' is first-person singular future-perfective of 'спросить' used in the protasis ('if I ask'), with 'его' as direct object ('him'). This expresses a hypothetical/sudden question the narrator might pose.
segments.p2-8-g618: Indirect/interrogative clause 'что ему надо?' functions as the content of the question. 'Что' = 'what'; 'ему' is dative ('to him'); 'надо' is an impersonal predicate meaning roughly 'is needed to him' — colloquially 'what he wants/needs'. The whole clause is an indirect question in Russian syntax.
segments.p2-8-g619: Pronoun 'он' (he) + negated perfective verb 'не ответит' (will not answer) plus 'ничего' ('anything/nothing') — perfective here indicates the decisive fact that he gives no reply. In narrative this is presented as a regular outcome.
segments.p2-8-g620: Present-tense verb 'продолжает' + infinitive 'смотреть' form the periphrastic present progressive 'continues to stare'; 'на меня' marks the object of the look, 'в упор' is an adverbial idiom meaning 'fixedly' or 'straight at (one's face)', and 'еще несколько секунд' gives the duration ('a few more seconds').
segments.p2-8-g621: Temporal connective adverb 'потом' meaning 'then' or 'after that', linking the sustained stare to the following actions.
segments.p2-8-g622: 'Как-то особенно' ('somehow/in a particular way, especially') modifies the gerund 'сжав' (perfective de-participle/gerund meaning 'having pressed' or 'pressing together'), which together with 'губы' describes the manner ('pressing his lips together') concurrent with the subsequent motion. The perfective gerund indicates a completed or punctual accompanying action.
segments.p2-8-g623: Preposition 'с' + instrumental 'видом' ('with an appearance/air') expresses manner: 'with a многозначительный (significant/expressive/meaningful) look/air'. The adjective describes the expressive quality of his bearing.
segments.p2-8-g624: Two coordinated perfective verbs in 3rd person singular: 'повернется' ('will turn') and 'уйдет' ('will leave'). 'На месте' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'on the spot' (i.e., without moving away first). Both verbs are modified by the adverb 'медленно' ('slowly'), indicating the manner and sequence of actions, and 'в свою комнату' is a motion-to phrase with 'в' + accusative showing destination.
segments.p2-8-g517: Adverb + verb + subject + object order here (Особенно бесил он меня). 'Особенно' is an adverb of degree ('especially/most of all') placed up front for emphasis; 'бесил' is the past-tense imperfective of бесить and indicates a repeated/habitual irritation ('infuriated me, used to infuriate me'). The inversion (verb before subject) gives added emphasis on the annoyance.
segments.p2-8-g518: 'когда' is the subordinating conjunction 'when', introducing the following time/clause; the trailing comma shows the clause will be parenthetical or qualified.
segments.p2-8-g519: 'бывало' is a parenthetical/impersonal past-form adverb meaning 'it would happen' or 'as often happened' and marks the action as habitual or recurring in the narrator's recollection.
segments.p2-8-g520: 'начнёт читать' is third-person singular (нaчнёт) + infinitive and literally means 'will begin to read'; with the preceding 'бывало' it expresses habitual past action ('would begin to read'). 'у себя за перегородкой' is a locative phrase: 'у себя' = 'on his side / at his place', 'за перегородкой' = 'behind the partition'. 'Псалтырь' is the direct object (accusative) naming the book read.
segments.p2-8-g521: 'Много битв' is a quantified noun phrase with 'много' + genitive plural ('битв') meaning 'many battles' or 'many struggles'. 'Вынес' is the past tense of вынести and here means 'to endure' or 'to put up with'. Word order (object-before-subject 'вынес я') is stylistic and common in Russian narratives.
segments.p2-8-g522: 'Но' = 'but'. 'Он ужасно любил читать' uses the past-tense любил (habitual liking) with 'ужасно' as an intensive adverb meaning 'very/terribly' (colloquial intensifier). 'По вечерам' is a time expression with по + dative plural meaning 'in the evenings' (habitual occurrence).
segments.p2-8-g523: 'тихим' is an adjective in the instrumental singular (agreeing with голосом) used adverbially: 'with a quiet ...'. The comma separates coordinate descriptive fragments.
segments.p2-8-g524: 'ровным голосом' is an instrumental phrase ('with a steady/even voice') modifying how he read; both words are in the instrumental to form an adverbial description of manner.
segments.p2-8-g525: 'нараспев' is an adverb meaning 'in a chant/intoned way' (literally 'to a tune'), used to describe the manner of reading.
segments.p2-8-g526: 'точно как' = 'just/as if', with 'точно' functioning as a comparative particle ('as if'); 'по мертвом' is an idiomatic/older colloquial expression meaning 'in the manner of funerals / addressed to the dead' (here 'по' + noun gives a manner/place sense). The whole phrase says his reading sounded like that used at funerals.
segments.p2-8-g527: 'Любопытно' is an impersonal adverb meaning 'it is curious/strange' or 'strangely enough', used as a comment introducing the surprising result that follows.
segments.p2-8-g528: 'что' introduces the content clause ('that he ...'); 'он тем и кончил' is an idiomatic clause: 'он кончил тем' = 'he ended/finished with that' (i.e., 'that is how he ended up'). Here 'кончил' means 'ended up/finished (in that way)' rather than 'died', and 'тем' refers back to the preceding behaviour.
segments.p2-8-g529: 'он теперь нанимается читать Псалтырь по покойникам' — 'нанимается' is reflexive present meaning 'he hires himself out' or 'takes work'; 'читать Псалтырь по покойникам' means 'to read the Psalter for/over the deceased' (i.e., performing funeral/commemoration readings). 'по' + dative plural here indicates the people concerned (the dead) and is a common collocation in funeral contexts.
segments.p2-8-g530: 'а вместе с тем' is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'and along with that / at the same time'. 'истребляет крыс' is present-tense 'he exterminates rats' (истребляет = destroys/eradicates); 'и делает ваксу' is colloquial 'and makes/applies wax (shoe-polish)' — a secondary occupation mentioned as happening concurrently.
segments.p2-8-g677: 'заревел' is the past tense, masculine singular of the perfective verb 'зареветь' used here as a simple past action ('I roared/shouted'); 'я' is the subject 'I' placed after the verb for emphatic or stylistic effect (in Russian subject-verb order is flexible). The comma shows the end of the narrative clause before the quoted imperative.
segments.p2-8-g678: 'подбегая' is the imperfective gerund (деепричастие) of 'подбегать' and indicates a concurrent action ('while/as I ran up'); 'к нему' is a dative/oblique phrase 'to him' showing direction toward the person. Together they set the manner/time of the main verb.
segments.p2-8-g679: This is the quoted imperative expression. 'ни' is an emphatic negative particle here ('not at all / not even'); 'с' is the preposition 'from' and 'места' is the genitive of 'место' ('place/spot'); the literal phrasing 'ни с места!' is idiomatic: 'don't move from the spot' → 'don't move an inch.'
segments.p2-8-g680: 'Так' is an adverb/response particle meaning 'so/thus' used here as a short, pointed reaction or connective comment to what just happened; as a one-word sentence it functions like an interjection ('I see' / 'very well / so').
segments.p2-8-g681: 'Отвечай' is the 2nd-person singular imperative of 'отвечать' ('Answer!'); 'теперь' is the adverb 'now' that limits the imperative. Together they form a direct command: 'Answer now.'
segments.p2-8-g682: 'Чего' is the colloquial/genitive form of 'что' used in questions and here functions as 'what/why' (i.e. 'what for' or 'what...'); 'ты' is the 2nd-person singular subject 'you'; 'входил' is the past imperfective of 'входить' ('you entered/were coming in' — here idiomatically 'you came in'); 'смотреть' is the infinitive complement 'to look/see' and completes the meaning 'come in to look (at)'. The word order places the interrogative and verb before the infinitive, producing the natural English: 'what did you come in to look at?'.
segments.p2-8-g683: 'Если' is the conditional conjunction 'if'; 'таперича' is a colloquial adverb meaning 'now/at present'; 'вам' is the dative 'to you' (polite/plural); 'есть' here functions existentially ('there is/you have'); 'что' is the interrogative/indefinite 'something/anything' (object of 'есть'); 'мне' is the dative 'to me'; 'приказать' is the perfective infinitive 'to order/command'. The clause literally asks 'If now you have anything to command me...' — Russian uses 'вам есть что мне приказать' with two datives surrounding 'есть что'.
segments.p2-8-g684: 'То' here is the result/then-particle 'then/so'; 'мое дело' is a fixed phrase 'my business/duty' with 'мое' agreeing in gender/number with 'дело'; 'исполнить' is the perfective infinitive 'to carry out/fulfill' and completes the clause: 'then it is my duty to carry it out.'
segments.p2-8-g685: 'отвечал' is the past tense of 'отвечать' ('he answered') and functions as the speech tag; 'он' is the pronoun 'he' (postposed here for the narrative rhythm). The dash and placement mark a typical Russian dialogic reporting style.
segments.p2-8-g686: 'опять-таки' is a colloquial adverbial combination meaning 'once again / again nonetheless' (opposes or reiterates the previous action); 'помолчав' is the perfective gerund (деепричастие) of 'помолчать' meaning 'having been silent' or 'after a pause.' Together they indicate he paused again before continuing.
segments.p2-8-g687: 'тихо и размеренно' are adverbial modifiers ('quietly and measuredly'); 'сюсюкая' is the imperfective gerund of the colloquial verb 'сюсюкать' meaning 'to coo/ to speak in a babyish/patronizing way' and describes the manner of his speaking simultaneously with the main verb.
segments.p2-8-g688: 'подняв' is a perfective gerund 'having raised'; 'брови' is the accusative plural 'eyebrows'; 'и' links the next participle; 'спокойно' is an adverb 'calmly'; 'перегнув' is another gerund from 'перегнуть' used here as 'having bent/tilted'; 'голову' is the direct object 'head'; 'с одного плеча на другое' is a prepositional phrase 'from one shoulder to the other' describing the motion. Both gerunds specify attendant bodily actions accompanying his speech.
segments.p2-8-g689: 'и' is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; 'все это' ('all this') is the noun phrase referring to the described behavior; 'с' is the preposition 'with' taking the instrumental; 'ужасающим' is the active participial/adjectival form meaning 'terrifying' (instrumental), and 'спокойствием' is the instrumental of 'calm/composure' — together an oxymoronic instrumental phrase 'with terrifying calm' that describes the overall manner.
segments.p2-8-g727: This is an impersonal, exclamatory phrase built around the infinitive 'Быть' plus the finite verb 'может' with negation. 'Быть' is the infinitive used idiomatically here as part of the fixed construction 'быть того не может' meaning 'that cannot be.' 'Того' is the genitive demonstrative (part of the phrase: 'of that' → 'that'), 'не' negates the verb, and 'может' is the 3rd person singular present of 'мочь' used impersonally to mean 'is possible' or 'can be.' The word order is emphatic/exclamatory rather than literal subject–predicate syntax.
segments.p2-8-g728: 'Отвечал он' places the verb before the pronoun — a common narrative inversion that emphasizes the action; 'отвечал' is past tense, imperfective, indicating he answered/replied. 'С' governs the instrumental case and means 'with.' 'Какою-то' is an older/formal instrumental feminine form of 'какой-то' ('some/a certain'), here modifying the following nominal phrase. 'Неестественною' is an instrumental singular feminine adjective in 19th-century spelling (modern: 'неестественной') meaning 'unnatural' or 'affected,' and it agrees with 'самоуверенностью' which is the instrumental singular noun 'self-confidence' (the instrument of his answering: 'with ...').
segments.p2-8-g729: 'Будет' is the 3rd person singular future of 'быть' used alone as an assertive/exclamatory predicate ('it will be' / 'there will be'). There is no explicit subject — the verb is used impersonally or elliptically to answer or pledge that something will happen.
segments.p2-8-g730: 'Кричал я' is past-tense narration with inverted word order (verb before pronoun) emphasizing the action: 'I shouted.' 'Кричал' is past imperfective, reporting the utterance rather than its completion.
segments.p2-8-g731: 'Даю' is 1st person singular present used performatively ('I give' = 'I swear' / 'I promise'); 'тебе' is the dative 'to you' (the addressee). 'Честное слово' is a noun phrase ('honest word') functioning as the direct object — idiomatically 'an honest word' = a promise — and 'мое' is the possessive 'my' placed after the noun in a somewhat emphatic/old-fashioned order.
segments.p2-8-g732: As above, 'будет' is the 3rd person singular future of 'быть' used as an emphatic, concluding tag to the promise: 'it will be' / 'it shall be.' Here it functions as the content of the promise rather than naming a subject.
segments.p2-8-g638: This is the protasis of a conditional: 'Если ... , то ...'. 'Если' = 'if'. 'я' is the subject. 'и этим' uses 'и' as 'even/also' and 'этим' in the instrumental meaning 'by this/with this'. 'всё ещё не вразумлялся' is past imperfective reflexive meaning 'was still not coming to his senses/was still not being made to understand' ('всё ещё' = 'still'). 'и продолжал бунтоваться' uses 'продолжал' + infinitive to say 'continued to rebel'.
segments.p2-8-g639: The apodosis begins with the correlative particle 'то' ('then'). 'он' is the subject and 'вдруг начнёт вздыхать' is a future imperfective verb meaning 'he will suddenly begin to sigh' — here it describes a habitual reaction.
segments.p2-8-g640: 'на меня глядя' is a deепричастный оборот (adverbial participle phrase) meaning 'looking at me' or 'while looking at me'; 'на' is the preposition 'at/toward', 'меня' is the accusative 'me', and 'глядя' is the present adverbial participle of 'глядеть'.
segments.p2-8-g641: A simple verb + adverb: 'вздыхать долго' — 'to sigh for a long time.' 'вздыхать' is imperfective (continuous/iterative), and 'долго' modifies duration.
segments.p2-8-g642: 'глубоко' is an adverb modifying 'вздыхать' and means 'deeply' or 'profoundly'; it stacks with 'долго' to describe the manner of the sighs.
segments.p2-8-g643: 'точно измеряя одним этим вздохом всю глубину моего нравственного падения' is a participial clause ('измеряя' = 'measuring' / 'as if measuring') that comments on how he sighed. 'точно' here functions as 'as if / precisely as', 'одним этим вздохом' ('by that one sigh', instrumental) indicates the means, and 'всю глубину моего нравственного падения' is the object phrase 'the whole depth of my moral fall' (metaphorical).
segments.p2-8-g644: Simple coordinating particle 'и' = 'and', linking the participial description to the following result clause.
segments.p2-8-g645: 'разумеется' is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally', introducing an expected consequence.
segments.p2-8-g646: 'кончалось наконец тем' is part of the idiom 'кончаться тем, что...' meaning 'to end up (resulting) in the fact that...'; 'кончалось' is imperfective past 'ended', 'наконец' = 'finally', and 'тем' is the demonstrative linking to the following clause.
segments.p2-8-g647: 'что он одолевал вполне' is the content of 'кончалось тем, что...' — 'that he quite/entirely overcame (me)'. 'одолевал' is past imperfective indicating repeated/habitual overcoming (he would prevail), and 'вполне' = 'completely'.
segments.p2-8-g648: Short main clause: 'я бесился' — 'I raged / I went mad'. 'беситься' is reflexive/imperfective past meaning to be furious or act wildly.
segments.p2-8-g649: Single verb 'кричал' ('I shouted') continuing the account of the speaker's outward behaviour; imperfective past tense.
segments.p2-8-g650: 'но то' begins a contrast clause: 'но' = 'but', and 'то' is a demonstrative referring forward to the thing in question ('that [thing]').
segments.p2-8-g651: 'об чем дело шло' is a relative clause meaning 'about what the matter was' or 'what it concerned' — 'об' + 'чем' = 'about what', 'дело шло' = 'the matter proceeded' / idiomatically 'the point was'.
segments.p2-8-g652: The conclusion: 'всё-таки принуждаем был исполнить.' 'всё-таки' = 'nevertheless/after all', and the phrase 'принуждаем был исполнить' is an older/periphrastic passive meaning '(I) was compelled/forced to perform' — equal in sense to the modern 'был принуждён исполнить'. 'исполнить' is the infinitive 'to carry out'.
segments.p2-8-g653: 'В этот же раз' = 'on this very occasion' ('в' + demonstrative + emphatic 'же' + 'раз' = time/occurrence). 'едва только начались' is the correlative 'hardly/barely ... when' or 'no sooner ... than' with 'начались' (past plural) 'began'. 'обыкновенные маневры "строгих взглядов"' = 'the usual maneuvers of strict/stern looks' (манёвр used figuratively).
segments.p2-8-g654: 'как' here introduces the immediate consequence 'as/when/as soon as ...' (correlative to 'едва только'). 'я тотчас же вышел из себя' — 'I immediately lost my temper' (выйти из себя = idiom 'to lose one's temper'); 'тотчас же' = 'at once/immediately' (the particle 'же' intensifies immediacy). 'и в бешенстве на него накинулся' = 'and, in a rage, attacked/sprung upon him' — 'накинулся' is perfective past 'pounced on/assailed'.
segments.p2-8-g655: 'Слишком уж' is an emphatic collocation meaning 'far too' or 'excessively'; 'я был' is simple past 'I was'. 'и без того' is an idiom meaning 'even without that' or 'to begin with / already', so the whole means 'I was already (even before that) excessively irritated' — 'раздражен' is a short adjective/participial adjective 'irritated'.
segments.p2-8-g656: Imperative interjection 'Стой!' = 'Stop!' or 'Halt!' — direct command; tone is abrupt and urgent.
segments.p2-8-g690: 'Не об этом' places the negation before the prepositional phrase for emphasis: 'не' negates, 'об' (about/concerning) requires the prepositional case 'этом' ('this'). With the dash it reads as a clipped, emphatic refusal: 'Not about this.'
segments.p2-8-g691: Full clause: present tense first-person 'спрашиваю' ('I ask') with the negated prepositional phrase fronted for emphasis: literally 'not about this I you ask' → 'I'm not asking you about this.' Word order (negated object before subject) stresses the contrast.
segments.p2-8-g692: Single vocative-like noun used as an insult addressed to the other person. No case marking change here in colloquial speech — it's simply shouted: 'палач!' ('executioner'/'tormentor').
segments.p2-8-g693: Past tense perfective 'закричал' ('cried out/shouted') with the subject 'я' following the verb (a common word-order inversion in Russian narration and direct speech). The dash marks direct speech/exclamation.
segments.p2-8-g694: Present-participial gerund 'трясясь' ('trembling') describes manner; 'от' + noun in genitive gives cause: 'от злобы' = 'from/because of anger' (i.e. 'trembling with anger').
segments.p2-8-g695: Future tense perfective 'скажу' ('I will tell') with indirect object 'тебе' ('to you') — 'I'll tell you.' The verb is committed/perfective, indicating a single finished utterance in the future.
segments.p2-8-g696: Vocative-like direct address again — a thrown insult 'палач' (see earlier). Short, sharp form used mid-sentence.
segments.p2-8-g697: 'сам' here is emphatic reflexive: 'I myself' or 'I'll tell you personally.' It intensifies the subject: 'Я скажу тебе, сам' → 'I'll tell you myself.'
segments.p2-8-g698: 'Для чего' = 'for what reason/why'; 'ты приходишь сюда' is present tense 'you come here.' The whole phrase asks the motive: 'for what reason do you come here?'
segments.p2-8-g699: 'Ты видишь' literally 'you see' used as a discourse marker meaning 'you can see/you notice' introducing the explanatory clause that follows.
segments.p2-8-g700: Subordinate clause introduced by 'что' ('that'): 'that I do not give you your pay.' 'Выдаю' is present imperfective here meaning 'give out/hand over' (wages); 'жалованья' = 'pay/salary' in genitive.
segments.p2-8-g701: 'Сам не хочешь' = 'you yourself don't want to' — 'сам' emphasizes the subject's own unwillingness; 'хочешь' present tense 'you want/wish.'
segments.p2-8-g702: 'По гордости' is a short adverbial phrase meaning 'out of pride' or 'because of pride'; 'по' here marks motive and 'гордости' is genitive of 'pride.'
segments.p2-8-g703: Two infinitives in apposition: 'поклониться' ('to bow') — 'попросить' ('to ask [for]'). The dash links them as alternate expressions of the same action (i.e. to humble oneself / beg).
segments.p2-8-g704: 'И для того приходишь' = 'and for that (reason) you come' where 'для того' points back to the motive just given; 'с своими глупыми взглядами' = 'with your stupid looks/gazes' (instrumental plural); 'меня наказывать' is the infinitive purpose: 'to punish me.'
segments.p2-8-g705: Another infinitive in the series: 'мучить' ('to torment'). It completes the coordinated list of hostile actions the speaker attributes to the addressee.
segments.p2-8-g706: This fragment reproduces a stammered form 'подозр-р-реваешь' — the author breaks the verb 'подозреваешь' ('you suspect') to imitate stuttering. Grammatically it's 2nd-person present ('you do not suspect'); 'не' negates it and 'и' links it to the prior clause.
segments.p2-8-g707: Again a sharp direct address 'палач' — repeated as the addressee of the accusation and rhetorical question.
segments.p2-8-g708: 'Как это глупо' is an exclamatory clause: 'how/what foolish this is' — 'как' = 'how', 'это' = 'this', 'глупо' = 'foolish.' It introduces the speaker's ironic evaluation.
segments.p2-8-g709: Repeated adverb 'глупо' ('foolishly'/'stupid') used as emphatic exclamation; repetition increases rhetorical force.
segments.p2-8-g710: Another repetition of the exclamatory 'глупо' — purely emphatic, piling on the condemnation.
segments.p2-8-g711: Yet another repetition for mounting emphasis; stylistically Dostoevsky often repeats a word to imitate frantic speech.
segments.p2-8-g712: Final exclamatory 'глупо!' — the form and stress close the outburst. Here the dictionary sense used is the adjective-derived evaluation 'foolish/stupid.'
segments.p2-8-g713: Past narration: 'Он было ... стал повертываться.' 'Было' here is the particle indicating a brief/attempted action (often rendering 'was about to' or 'for a moment'), 'молча' = 'silently,' 'опять' = 'again,' 'стал' + infinitive = 'began to,' and 'повертываться' = 'to turn (oneself) around.'
segments.p2-8-g714: 'Но' = 'but/however' introduces the opposing action; 'я ухватил его' is simple past perfective: 'I seized/grabbed him' — 'его' is the accusative object 'him.'
segments.p2-8-g715: 'Слушай' is the 2nd-person singular imperative of слышать, an informal command meaning 'listen' (addressing one person). The leading dash signals this is direct speech.
segments.p2-8-g716: Past-tense imperfective verb 'кричал' (I shouted/was shouting) with the subject 'я' and the dative 'ему' ('to him'). The imperfective past here describes the act of shouting in the narrative; word order is standard S-V-(IO).
segments.p2-8-g717: 'Вот' is a demonstrative/attention particle meaning 'here/there' used to present something; 'деньги' is plural nominative 'money' (the object being shown). Together they mean 'here is the money.'
segments.p2-8-g718: 'Видишь' is 2nd-person singular present of видеть used as a conversational tag 'you see' (literally 'you see?'), commenting on the preceding gesture.
segments.p2-8-g719: Again 'вот' (here/there) plus the pronoun 'они' (they/those) referring back to the money: 'here they are.' It's a demonstrative confirmation.
segments.p2-8-g720: The parenthesis contains the clause with subject 'я' and perfective past 'вынул' ('I took out'), object 'их' ('them'), and the prepositional phrase 'из столика' ('out of the little table'; 'из' governs genitive 'столика'). 'все семь рублей' is an appositive specifying the total amount: 'all seven rubles' (numeral + genitive 'рублей').
segments.p2-8-g721: 'Но' is the adversative 'but'; 'ты' is the 2nd-person singular subject; 'их' is the accusative object 'them'; 'не получишь' is 2nd-person singular future (perfective) 'you will not receive/get.' Together: 'but you will not get them.'
segments.p2-8-g722: This line reproduces a stammer: 'не па-алучишь' is a broken/stammered form of 'не получишь' (you will not receive). The negation 'не' scopes the verb; 'до тех самых пор' is the fixed phrase 'until those very moments' = 'until the very moment.' Note: 'па-алучишь' has no independent lexical meaning here—it's phonetic stuttering.
segments.p2-8-g723: 'Пока не придешь' is a temporal clause meaning 'until you come/arrive' (пока…не + future verb: standard Russian 'until' construction). 'Почтительно' is an adverb 'respectfully' modifying the coming/arrival.
segments.p2-8-g724: 'С' + instrumental 'повинной головой' ('with a виновный/penitent head') is an idiomatic description: instrumental case expresses the manner—'with a penitent/contrite head,' i.e., humbly/contritely. 'Повинной' here describes a contrite or guilty attitude rather than a literal sick head.
segments.p2-8-g725: Infinitive 'просить' ('to ask/beg') governed by the preceding clause: 'to come ... to ask/beg.' 'У меня' is the standard phrase 'from me' (u + genitive) indicating the source/person at whom the request is directed; 'прощения' is the genitive of 'прощение' ('forgiveness') used with просить (просить прощения = to ask forgiveness).
segments.p2-8-g726: 'Слышал' is the past-tense form of слышать, here used as a brusque exclamation meaning 'Do you hear?' or 'Hear that!' — a colloquial interjection rather than a literal past action. Context determines it as an imperative-like prompt.
segments.p2-8-g563: Тогда is a sequence adverb meaning 'then' (marks what follows). выну is the perfective future, 1st person singular of вынуть ('I will take out/draw out'). все семь рублей is a quantified noun phrase where все ('all') modifies the numeral семь; рублей is genitive plural used with numerals. из ящика is a prepositional phrase ('out of the drawer') with ящика in genitive after из.
segments.p2-8-g564: покажу is the perfective future 1st person of показать ('I will show'); ему is the dative pronoun 'to him', the indirect object of покажу.
segments.p2-8-g565: что introduces a content clause ('that ...'). они у меня есть uses the existential/possessive construction у + genitive + есть to mean 'I have them' or 'they are with me' (есть here is the copula/existential). и нарочно отложены is a coordinated predicate: нарочно (adverb 'deliberately') + отложены (short passive participle, 'set aside').
segments.p2-8-g566: но что continues the reported-clause sequence ('but that ...'). я не хочу is a present indicative clause ('I do not want'), with не marking negation; the opening quote begins a direct reported utterance.
segments.p2-8-g567: Repeated не хочу — simple repetition for emphasis; same present-tense negated verb 'I do not want'.
segments.p2-8-g568: просто is an intensifying adverb ('simply/merely'). не хочу + infinitive выдать forms a negative desire construction 'do not want to hand over'; выдать is the perfective infinitive 'to give out/issue' (here: to pay out). ему is dative 'to him'; жалованье is the direct object 'salary/pay'.
segments.p2-8-g569: Another repetition of the negated present 'не хочу' for rhetorical insistence within the quoted refusal.
segments.p2-8-g570: потому что introduces a causal clause ('because'). так хочу is an idiomatic, colloquial phrase meaning roughly 'that's how I want it' or 'I want it that way' (хочу in present). The closing quote ends the reported utterance.
segments.p2-8-g571: Another causal clause: потому что 'because'; на это ('with regard to that' / 'for that') uses на + accusative to indicate what the reason applies to; the quoted phrase моя воля господская is a nominal phrase meaning 'my lordly/master's will' (господская is an adjective modifying воля).
segments.p2-8-g572: потому что again introduces a reason: он непочтителен uses the short-form adjective непочтителен as a predicate ('he is disrespectful'). Short-form adjectives serve as present-state predicates.
segments.p2-8-g573: Another causal clause: он грубиян — noun predicate 'he is a boor/lout' giving another reason for refusal; грубиян is a noun denoting a rude person.
segments.p2-8-g574: но что если introduces a conditional ('but that if ...'). если он попросит is a future (perfective) 3rd-person verb meaning 'if he asks (for it)'; почтительно is an adverb modifying попросит ('respectfully').
segments.p2-8-g575: то is the correlative particle matching если ('then'); я is the subject 'I' of the consequent clause.
segments.p2-8-g576: пожалуй is a parenthetical particle meaning 'perhaps' or softening the statement; it modifies the speaker's commitment to the following action.
segments.p2-8-g577: смягчусь is perfective future 1st person reflexive of смягчить/смягчиться ('I will soften/relent'); и дам coordinates another future verb: дам ('I will give'). Both are 1st-person future actions promised as possible consequences.
segments.p2-8-g578: не то ... прождет is an idiomatic turn meaning 'otherwise / if not, (he) will wait ...'. еще две недели indicates 'two more weeks' (еще = 'more/another'); недели is genitive plural after the numeral; прождет is future 3rd person singular 'will wait (a long time)'.
segments.p2-8-g579: Elliptical clause: the numeral 'три' (weeks) is implied; прождет is the same future 3rd-person verb 'will wait'; the omitted noun (недели) is understood from the previous phrase.
segments.p2-8-g580: целый месяц is an emphatic time expression ('a whole month'); целый is an adjective intensifier. прождет again is future 3rd person 'will wait' — giving the maximal delay.
segments.p2-8-g583: Но introduces contrast ('but/however'); как ни был зол is a concessive construction ('however/whatever I was angry'), using как + ни + past tense был to mean 'no matter how...'; зол is the short-form adjective 'angry' used predicatively.
segments.p2-8-g584: а все-таки is an adversative connective meaning 'and yet / nevertheless' (все-таки is a fixed adverbial phrase). он победил is a simple past, 3rd person singular: 'he won / he defeated (me).' The clause states the outcome despite the prior concession.
segments.p2-8-g585: Я is subject 'I'. и here functions as an intensifier meaning 'even' (common in colloquial/elliptical negative constructions). четырех дней is a genitive-quantified time expression 'four days' (genitive after the negative); не выдержал is past tense perfective 'did not endure / did not hold out'.
segments.p2-8-g586: Он начал с того is a simple past clause: начал is past masculine of начать ('began'), and с того is a fixed locution 'with that/with the following'.
segments.p2-8-g587: с чего всегда начинал is a relative clause ('with what he always started'), where чего is a genitive relative pronoun after с; всегда + начинал (imperfective past) indicates habitual/repeated past action; в подобных случаях is a locative phrase 'in similar/such cases'.
segments.p2-8-g588: потому что introduces a causal explanation: подобные случаи уже бывали uses уже ('already') and бывали (imperfective past of бывать) to mean 'such cases had already happened/occurred'.
segments.p2-8-g589: пробовались is a reflexive/imperfective verb here meaning 'were tried/attempted' or 'had been tried out' — it echoes бывали to stress that the same pattern had been tested before. The following и links the parenthetical remark.
segments.p2-8-g590: замечу is a parenthetical first-person verb 'I remark/I should note' (1st person, future-like present used for an authorial aside).
segments.p2-8-g591: я знал все это заранее — past-tense statement 'I knew all this in advance': знал is past of знать; все это is 'all this'; заранее is an adverb 'beforehand / in advance.'
segments.p2-8-g592: я знал наизусть его подлую тактику repeats 'I knew' with the adverb наизусть ('by heart'), his (его) modifies подлую тактику ('vile/mean tactic'); подлую is an accusative feminine adjective agreeing with тактику.
segments.p2-8-g593: именно is a focusing particle 'namely' or 'that is to say', introducing the precise description to follow.
segments.p2-8-g594: он начинал с того repeats the frame 'he would begin with that' (начинал imperfective past indicating habitual action).
segments.p2-8-g595: что устремит: что introduces the subordinate clause; устремит is perfective future 3rd person of устремить ('he will direct/aim'), here used about directing a look (i.e., 'he would fix/direct').
segments.p2-8-g596: бывало is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'as used to happen' or 'it would happen' — it marks that what follows was a habitual occurrence in the past.
segments.p2-8-g597: на меня is a directional prepositional phrase 'at me' (literally 'on me'); чрезвычайно строгий взгляд is a noun phrase 'an extremely severe look' (чрезвычайно intensifies the adjective строгий); взгляд is the direct object of устремит.
segments.p2-8-g598: не спускает его is a present-tense habitual statement 'he does not lower it / does not take it off' (i.e., does not avert his gaze); несколько минут сряду is a duration expression 'for several minutes in a row', where минут is genitive plural and сряду ('in a row') specifies continuity.
segments.p2-8-g599: особенно introduces a nuance 'especially'; встречая and провожая are present-gerund (деепричастие) forms meaning 'when meeting' and 'when seeing off' respectively, describing the occasions when he fixed his gaze; из дому is an idiomatic phrase 'from home / out of the house'.
segments.p2-8-g744: This short exclamation has no copula: 'А' is a discourse particle (here introducing an emphatic remark), 'ты' is the 2nd-person subject, and the compound particle 'все-таки' (все + таки) adds the sense 'after all/still' to the predicate noun 'палач' (used accusatively/vocatively as an angry label). The sentence omits any verb (a common Russian structure for sharp exclamations), so the noun functions as a predicate/name applied to 'ты'.
segments.p2-8-g745: Single-word exclamation: the noun is used as an address/accusation (vocative sense) with strong emotional force. No additional morphology or verb is present; intonation and punctuation supply the force.
segments.p2-8-g746: A repetition of the previous exclamation; again the bare noun serves as an accusatory shout addressed to the person, functioning without a verb.
segments.p2-8-g747: 'Но' is the adversative conjunction 'but'; 'он' is the 3rd-person pronoun subject; 'только' here means 'only/just' and limits the action; 'посмотрел' is past tense, perfective, 'looked (at)'; 'на меня' is the prepositional phrase marking the object ('at me'). Together they form a short past-action clause.
segments.p2-8-g748: 'затем' is an adverb meaning 'then/afterwards'; 'повернулся' is past tense, perfective, reflexive verb 'turned (around)'; 'и' is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking this action to the following participial clause and verb.
segments.p2-8-g749: This is an adverbial participle phrase: 'уже не слушая' uses 'уже' with the negated gerund 'не слушая' to mean 'no longer listening' (i.e., he stopped heeding). 'призывных' is an adjective 'imploring/appealing' modifying 'криков' (genitive plural 'of cries'), and 'моих' is the possessive 'my' placed after the noun in the older/poetic order 'криков моих' ('my cries'). The whole phrase modifies his following action.
segments.p2-8-g750: 'плавно' is an adverb of manner 'smoothly/calmly'; 'пошел' is past tense, perfective 'went/started to move'; 'к' is the preposition 'to/toward'; 'себе' (from 'себя') here means 'to his own place' ('to himself') indicating motion toward his own quarters. The clause indicates the manner and direction of his movement.
segments.p2-8-g751: Another adverbial gerund 'не оборачиваясь' ('not turning around' / 'without looking back'): negation + gerund forms a manner clause describing that he did not look back as he left. The reflexive verb indicates turning oneself.
segments.p2-8-g805: В эту минуту is a temporal accusative phrase (в + accusative) meaning 'at this moment' or 'at that instant'; эту agrees with минуту (feminine accusative singular). Мои часы is the nominative plural subject ('my watch/clock' uses plural form часы), and принатужились is past-tense, reflexive, plural—the verb literally means 'strained themselves' or 'made an effort' and agrees with the plural subject; it anthropomorphizes the clock and leads into the coordinated actions in the next segment.
segments.p2-8-g806: Прошипели is past plural (agreeing with часы) describing a hissing sound produced by the mechanism; и is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; пробили is also past plural and here means 'struck' (of a clock striking the hour). The numeral семь functions as the accusative object indicating which hour was struck ('struck seven').
segments.p2-8-g752: "Если б" is a colloquial contraction of "если бы" and marks a counterfactual conditional ('if ... were/had'). The particle "б" (short for "бы") attaches to the conditional clause; "не Лиза" literally 'not Liza' (= 'if it were not for Liza'). The clause omits any copula — typical in Russian conditionals where the existence/result is expressed in the main clause.
segments.p2-8-g753: "не было б" combines the past tense of быть (было) with the conditional particle бы to form a counterfactual meaning 'would not have been/would not exist'. "ничего этого" is a genitive phrase 'nothing of this' (the genitive after "ничего"); together it yields 'there would be none of this.' Word order places the negated verb phrase before the genitive object for emphasis.
segments.p2-8-g754: "решил" is the masculine past tense of "решить" ('decide, resolve'), and "я" is the subject. The inversion "решил я" (verb before pronoun) gives a slightly more emphatic or narrative tone. "про себя" is an idiom meaning 'to oneself / in one's mind', indicating an inward thought rather than a spoken remark.
segments.p2-8-g755: "Затем" is an adverb meaning 'then' or 'afterwards' and sets the temporal sequence before the following participial clause.
segments.p2-8-g756: "постояв" is a perfective adverbial participle (деепричастие) from "постоять", meaning 'having stood' or 'after standing'; it denotes a brief, completed action that precedes the main verb. "с минуту" is a colloquial duration phrase 'for about a minute' ("с" + accusative expresses duration here).
segments.p2-8-g757: "важно и торжественно" are adverbial/phraseive modifiers describing the manner of his stance or bearing: "важно" here conveys an air of self-importance or 'with importance', and "торжественно" means 'solemnly' or 'with ceremony'. They modify the implied manner of the subject before the contrasting clause.
segments.p2-8-g758: "но" ('but') introduces a contrast between the outward bearing and inner physical state. "с" ('with') governs the instrumental phrase "медленно и сильно бьющимся сердцем": "медленно" and "сильно" are adverbs modifying the present active participle "бьющимся" ('beating'), and "сердцем" is in the instrumental case showing the accompaniment/condition ('with a slowly and strongly beating heart').
segments.p2-8-g759: "я отправился" is the past reflexive verb "отправиться" meaning 'set off' or 'proceeded' (here 'I went'). "сам" is emphatic 'myself' / 'personally'. "к нему" ('to him') uses the dative ("к + dative") to indicate movement toward a person. "за ширмы" uses "за" + accusative to indicate motion to the place 'behind the screens' (i.e., going to the other side of the folding screens).
segments.p2-8-g760: A one-word exclamation in the nominative; Russian uses the nominative to call or exclaim names. Depending on context this can be a literal invocation of the god or an ironic/metaphorical address meaning 'handsome man' — used here as an exclamation naming or praising someone.
segments.p2-8-g801: Он is the masculine singular subject. не хотел уходить is a past-tense imperfective construction meaning “did not want to leave” (imperfective хочется/хотеть expresses an ongoing or habitual desire; here it shows reluctance). и coordinates a second predicate; насмешливо is an adverb modifying рассматривал; нас is the accusative pronoun “us” (direct object of рассматривал). Рассматривал is past imperfective, masculine singular, indicating a continuing or repeated action “looked/examined.”
segments.p2-8-g802: Ступай is the imperative form (colloquial) of ступать ‘to step/go,’ used here as a direct command. The dash and exclamation mark mark it as reported speech and a sharp order.
segments.p2-8-g803: «ступай» is the 2nd-person singular imperative of the imperfective verb ступать ('to step, to set out'). Here it functions as a brusque verbal command telling someone to move off or leave; the exclamation mark intensifies the order. Because it's imperfective, it focuses on the act of moving rather than its completion.
segments.p2-8-g804: командовал is the past tense, 1st-person singular form of командовать (imperfective), here meaning 'I gave an order' or 'I ordered'. The subject pronoun я follows the verb for colloquial emphasis (in Russian word order is flexible). ему is a dative pronoun marking the recipient of the command ('to him'). потерявшись is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'having lost one's way' or 'being at a loss' and modifies the main verb, indicating the attendant circumstance under which the ordering took place; stylistically the clause omits a comma but still reads as 'I ordered him, having lost my way.'
segments.p2-8-g733: И is a discourse connective ('and') introducing the speaker's turn. Не в чем ... просить is an impersonal construction meaning 'there is nothing (in which) to ask'; мне (dative) marks the experiencer/beneficiary ('for me') and у вас ('from you/at you') marks the source/target of the request; the infinitive просить completes the construction ('to ask/request'). Word order is stylistically fronted but grammatical: 'there is nothing for me to ask from you'.
segments.p2-8-g734: Продолжал is past imperfective ('he continued/kept on') describing an ongoing action in the past; он is the subject placed after the verb (inversion common in narrative). The phrase marks a shift back to the reported speech.
segments.p2-8-g735: Как бы (with the particle бы) here functions as a softened simile—'as if' or 'as though.' Совсем не замечая is a gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'not noticing at all' and supplies a circumstantial adverbial to the main verb (продолжал). Моих криков is genitive ('of my shouts/cries').
segments.p2-8-g736: Потому begins the causal clause ('because/for that reason'). Вы же uses вы (formal/plural 'you') plus the emphatic particle же ('indeed/after all') stressing the accusation. Обозвали is perfective past ('called/named (someone)') and takes the accusative меня plus the instrumental палачом to mean 'called me (by the name) "executioner"' (Russian uses the instrumental to indicate naming someone as something).
segments.p2-8-g737: На чем here introduces the consequence/basis ('for which' / 'on that account'). Я с вас могу просить literally 'I can ask from you'; в сочетании с в квартале всегда за обиду просить it means 'I can always demand from you, in the quarter/neighborhood, for the insult'—i.e. exact compensation or satisfaction. Note с here = 'from' and за обиду = 'for (because of) the insult'; the modal могу precedes the infinitive просить.
segments.p2-8-g738: Иди is the second-person singular imperative of идти ('go'); the dash and exclamation mark mark direct, sharp command addressed to the interlocutor.
segments.p2-8-g739: Проси is the second-person singular imperative of просить ('ask/request' or 'beg'). In context it is an imperative encouraging or challenging the other to 'ask' (for forgiveness, compensation, etc.).
segments.p2-8-g740: Заревел is a past perfective verb meaning 'broke into a roar/shouted out' (a sudden, loud outburst); я is the subject following the verb for emphasis/inversion. The verb conveys an impassioned exclamation.
segments.p2-8-g741: Иди is the imperative 'go'; сейчас is an adverb meaning 'now' or 'immediately'—together they form a blunt command for immediate departure.
segments.p2-8-g742: Сию минуту is an emphatic, somewhat archaic phrase meaning 'this very minute' (сию = demonstrative 'this' in an older/style register); минуту is accusative of минута used adverbially as 'minute/moment.'
segments.p2-8-g743: Сию секунду repeats the same archaic demonstrative plus a shorter time unit: 'this very second'—an even stronger immediate demand. The exclamation mark emphasizes urgency.
segments.p2-8-g761: «сказал» is a past-tense, masculine, perfective verb; the subject «я» follows the verb (inverted order after the dash) for emphasis. «тихо и с расстановкой» are adverbial modifiers: «тихо» is an adverb 'quietly', while «с расстановкой» is a prepositional phrase (с + instrumental) functioning like an adverbial idiom meaning 'with measured composure'.
segments.p2-8-g762: «но» is the conjunction 'but'. «задыхаясь» is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) from «задыхаться» meaning 'being breathless' or 'gasping', and it indicates a concurrent circumstance (he says it while out of breath).
segments.p2-8-g763: «сходи» is the singular imperative of «сходить» ('go' or 'go and come back/fetch'); «тотчас же» is an emphatic time phrase 'at once' (тотчас + emphatic particle же). «и нимало не медля» is a linked adverbial phrase: «и» connects the phrase, «нимало не медля» (literally 'not at all hesitating') uses the gerund-like form «медля» with negation to mean 'without any delay'. «за квартальным надзирателем» uses the preposition «за» + instrumental to mean 'to fetch/for' the named official; «квартальным надзирателем» names the official (квартальный надзиратель = district/quarter inspector).
segments.p2-8-g764: «Он» is the pronoun 'he'. «было» here is the past-tense particle often used with verbs to indicate a brief/attempted or already completed action (can be left untranslated). «уж уселся» is perfective past 'had sat down' (усесться). «тем временем» is the fixed adverbial phrase 'in the meantime / by that time'. «за своим столом» (за + instrumental) means 'at his desk' (literally 'behind his table'), with «своим» marking possession.
segments.p2-8-g765: «надел очки» is past-tense action 'put on (his) glasses' (надел + direct object). «и взял что-то шить» links another past action: «взял» + infinitive «шить» means 'took up to sew' or 'began sewing something'; «что-то» is the indefinite pronoun 'something' acting as the object of «шить».
segments.p2-8-g767: «услышав» is a perfective adverbial participle (деепричастие) from «услышать», meaning 'having heard' and indicating a prior/causal circumstance. «мое приказанье» is the accusative object 'my order/command' (мое = 'my', приказанье = 'order').
segments.p2-8-g768: «вдруг» is an adverb 'suddenly'. «фыркнул» is past masculine perfective 'snorted' (derisive sound). «со смеху» is a colloquial phrase using the variant preposition «со» + noun in the dative/archaic form meaning 'with laughter/derision' (here indicating derisive laughter).
segments.p2-8-g769: «Сейчас» is an adverb meaning 'now' or 'immediately' and here serves as an urgent temporal adverb introducing the command.
segments.p2-8-g770: «сию минуту» is an older or emphatic accusative time-expression meaning 'this very minute' (сию = archaic/emphatic 'this'). «иди» is the second-person singular imperative of «идти» — 'go'. The time expression is fronted for force ('go this very minute').
segments.p2-8-g771: Иди is the second-person singular imperative of идти, a direct command addressed to one person. The short form with no subject is typical for spoken orders and serves as the main clause that the rest of the sentence warns about.
segments.p2-8-g772: Или introduces an alternative/colloquial warning ('or...'); ты is the familiar singular pronoun. И here functions as an emphatic particle = 'even'/'also' (not a simple 'and'), не negates the verb, and воображаешь is present indicative, second-person singular of воображать meaning 'you imagine'. Together this clause says 'or you don't even imagine'.
segments.p2-8-g773: Что introduces a subordinate clause ('what/that') and будет is the future tense, third-person singular of быть, meaning 'will be' or idiomatically 'will happen' here; the two together form the object clause 'what will happen'.
segments.p2-8-g774: Подлинно is a somewhat formal/old-fashioned adverb meaning 'truly' or 'indeed'. Вы is the polite/plural 'you'. The phrase не в своем уме is an idiom ('not in one's right mind'), built with в + possessive (своем) + noun (уме).
segments.p2-8-g775: Заметил is the perfective past tense, third-person singular of заметить meaning 'remarked' (here parenthetical). Он is the subject 'he'. The verb-before-subject order gives a narrative, reported-speech feel.
segments.p2-8-g776: Даже 'even' adds emphasis. Не + подняв is a negated perfective gerund (деепричастие) from поднять meaning 'having raised'; together non-literally 'without having raised (his) head'. Головы is the genitive singular of голова, the object of the gerund (Russian gerund often uses the genitive for direct object).
segments.p2-8-g777: Так же (так + же) here means 'just as' / 'in the same way' and медленно is the adverb 'slowly'. Сюсюкая is an imperfective gerund meaning 'cooing' or talking in a babyish/patronizing way; and продолжая is another imperfective gerund 'continuing'. Вдевать нитку is the verb+object 'to thread the thread' (вдевать takes the thread in the accusative), showing the concurrent action he continues while speaking.
segments.p2-8-g778: И is a simple connective particle ('and'/'well'). Где это видано is a fixed, colloquial/exclamatory expression meaning 'where has this been seen?' or 'when/where ever has that happened?'; видано here is an impersonal past form used in exclamation rather than a literal past participle.
segments.p2-8-g779: Чтоб (short form of чтобы) introduces the content clause 'that...'. Человек = 'person/man'. Сам is emphatic/reflexive here meaning 'himself' or 'of his own accord'. Против себя = 'against himself' (против + accusative себя). За + начальством with начальством in the instrumental indicates the authorities as the object toward whom one goes; ходил is the imperfective past ('would go' / 'went about') expressing the idea of such an action happening. The whole clause asks rhetorically whether a person would go to the authorities contrary to his own interest.
segments.p2-8-g780: А is a contrastive conjunction ('and' / 'but'). Касательно means 'regarding' or 'as for'; here it is followed colloquially by страху (dative) to mean 'as for fear' or 'with regard to fear'.
segments.p2-8-g781: Напрасно means 'in vain' or 'needlessly'. Только here means 'only' / 'merely' (restrictive). Надсажаетесь is second-person plural present reflexive of надсаживать/надсадить, meaning literally 'you strain (your voice)' or more broadly 'you overstrain/strain yourselves'—figuratively 'making a fuss' or 'straining your voice'. The plural form (implied вы) addresses the group or uses polite form.
segments.p2-8-g782: Потому is causal ('therefore' / 'for that reason'). Ничего не будет is a common impersonal negated future construction meaning 'nothing will be' or idiomatically 'nothing will come of it' / 'nothing will happen'; note the negation не appears before the verb and ничего is the negative pronoun 'nothing'.
segments.p2-8-g791: Я is the nominative personal pronoun 'I' (subject). взглянул is the past tense, perfective, masculine singular form of взглянуть ('to glance'), so together this is a simple past action: 'I glanced.' The comma separates this short action from the immediately following reactions.
segments.p2-8-g792: обмер is past masculine singular of (об)мереть/обмер — here meaning 'went still' or 'was frozen' (sudden cessation of movement) caused by an emotion; со + genitive (со стыда) indicates cause/source, 'from/with shame.' The coordinating и links this state to бросился, where бросился is past reflexive perfective 'hurried/rushed (oneself)' and в свою комнату uses в + accusative for motion into, with свою 'one's own' agreeing with комнату in accusative feminine.
segments.p2-8-g793: Там is an adverb of place 'there' and locates the action that follows; here it fronted for emphasis before the participial clause.
segments.p2-8-g794: схватив is a perfective adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'having grabbed/seizing' and introduces a subordinate action simultaneous with the main clause. себя is the reflexive accusative 'himself/myself' (object of схватив). обеими руками is instrumental plural 'with both hands' specifying the instrument, and за волосы uses за + accusative to indicate where he grabbed ('by/at the hair'). The whole participial phrase modifies the main verb in the next segment.
segments.p2-8-g795: я is the subject 'I'. прислонился is past reflexive perfective 'leaned/pressed (myself) against'; головой is instrumental singular indicating the body part used ('with my head'); к + dative (к стене) marks the target 'to/against the wall.' и connects to замер, where замер is past 'became motionless/froze' (stood still), and в этом положении is a locative phrase 'in this position' describing the state he remained in.
segments.p2-8-g796: Минуты через две is a temporal expression meaning 'in about two minutes' or 'two minutes later' (через + accusative expresses elapsed time). послышались is the past, impersonal/compound reflexive verb 'were heard' (it became audible), agreeing in sense with the following plural subject. медленные шаги is the nominative plural subject 'slow steps/footsteps,' and Аполлона is in the genitive indicating whose steps they are ('Apollo's' / 'Apollon's'). Note the time phrase is fronted for narrative effect.
segments.p2-8-g797: Там is 'there' (locative). какая-то is the indefinite phrase 'some/one of some kind' (какая + то = 'some'), functioning as the subject 'somebody/some person.' вас is the accusative 'you' (object of спрашивает). спрашивает is present tense, 3rd person singular 'is asking' (or 'asks'), so the literal structure is 'There some one is asking you.'
segments.p2-8-g798: сказал is the past tense, masculine singular form 'he said' and functions as the speech tag; он is the pronoun 'he' in nominative, included for clarity/emphasis but allow the typical inversion after reported speech.
segments.p2-8-g799: особенно строго is an adverbial phrase 'especially sternly' describing manner; смотря is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) of смотреть meaning 'looking' and here describes how he said it (concurrent action); на меня is the prepositional phrase 'at me' indicating the target of his look.
segments.p2-8-g800: потом 'then/later' sequences the actions; посторонился is past reflexive 'stepped aside' (moved out of the way); и 'and' links the verbs; пропустил is past 'let through/permitted to pass' and the dash before Лизу highlights the direct object (the name) being admitted—Лизу is accusative 'Liza' (the person allowed to pass).
segments.p2-8-g783: "Иди!" is the 2nd-person singular imperative of идти, a direct command telling someone to go away; the dash marks direct speech. It is colloquial and immediate, with the verb standing alone as the whole utterance.
segments.p2-8-g784: The word order places the past-tense verb визжал before the subject я for emphasis; визжал is past tense (masculine speaker) and expresses a high, sharp cry. The narrator uses the simple past to report a single past action.
segments.p2-8-g785: Хватая is a деепричастие (adverbial participle/gerund) formed from the imperfective хватать and indicates a simultaneous, ongoing action ('grabbing' / 'seizing') that accompanies the main verb. Его is the accusative object pronoun 'him,' and за плечо is the phrase marking what part was grabbed; плечо is in the accusative. Together they describe how the shriek was produced (while seizing him by the shoulder).
segments.p2-8-g786: Чувствовал is the past tense, imperfective of чувствовать and here means 'I felt' (an internal feeling/awareness). The subject pronoun Я is explicit and typical for emphasis; the imperfective frames the sensation as a continuing state preceding the subordinate clause.
segments.p2-8-g787: The subordinate clause introduced by что ('that') contains сейчас ('at once/just now') and the perfective future ударю ('I will strike/hit' — 1st person singular, indicating a single completed action about to occur). Его is the object 'him.' The aspect contrast (imperfective felt → perfective will hit) expresses an imminent, single action the speaker anticipates.
segments.p2-8-g788: Но introduces a contrast ('but'); я is the subject; the particle и after the subject gives emphatic force ('didn't even'); не слыхал is past tense negative ('didn't hear' / 'hadn't heard'). The phrase as a whole stresses that the narrator did not notice the event audibly.
segments.p2-8-g789: Как here functions as a temporal conjunction 'how/when' ('how, at that moment'), в это мгновение is a time phrase ('at that moment') with в + accusative used for a point in time, and вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly.' Дверь (feminine) is the subject; из сеней ('out of the vestibule') uses из + genitive to mark origin; тихо и медленно are adverbs modifying the verb. Отворилась is a past-tense, intransitive verb meaning 'opened' — an older/less common variant of открылась — and agrees in gender/number with дверь. Какая-то фигура is an indefinite expression ('some figure/person') where то is the indefinite particle; вошла is the past feminine of войти 'entered.'
segments.p2-8-g790: Остановилась is past tense (feminine) 'stopped/paused' referring to the figure; и links to the next verb. С недоумением uses the preposition с + instrumental (недоумением) to indicate manner ('with perplexity/bewilderment'). Начала is past tense feminine of начать ('began') and is followed by the imperfective разглядывать ('to look over/examine'), which takes нас ('us') as its accusative object. The sequence describes the figure pausing and then beginning to scrutinize the group, in a puzzled manner.
